def max_removals(playlist, favorite_sequence, removable_indices)
  def is_subsequence(s, t, removed)
    i = 0
    j = 0
    while i < s.length && j < t.length
      if !removed[j] && s[i] == t[j]
        i += 1
      end
      j += 1
    end
    i == s.length
  end
  
  left = 0
  right = removable_indices.length
  
  while left < right
    mid = (left + right + 1) / 2
    removed = Array.new(playlist.length, false)
    
    (0...mid).each do |i|
      removed[removable_indices[i]] = true
    end
    
    if is_subsequence(favorite_sequence, playlist, removed)
      left = mid
    else
      right = mid - 1
    end
  end
  
  left
end

def main
  playlist = gets.chomp
  favorite_sequence = gets.chomp
  removable_indices = gets.chomp.split.map(&:to_i)
  
  result = max_removals(playlist, favorite_sequence, removable_indices)
  puts result
end

def solve()
  input_data = $stdin.read.split
  return if input_data.empty?
  
  playlist = input_data[0]
  favoriteSequence = input_data[1]
  removableIndices = input_data[2..-1].map(&:to_i)

  n = playlist.length
  m = favoriteSequence.length
  
  is_removable = Array.new(n, false)
  removableIndices.each { |idx| is_removable[idx] = true }

  dp = Array.new(m + 1, -Float::INFINITY)
  dp[0] = 0

  (0...n).each do |i|
    new_dp = dp.dup
    cost = is_removable[i] ? 1 : 0
    
    (0...m).each do |j|
      if playlist[i] == favoriteSequence[j]
        if dp[j] != -Float::INFINITY
          new_dp[j + 1] = [new_dp[j + 1], dp[j]].max
        end
      end
    end
    
    (0..m).each do |j|
      if dp[j] != -Float::INFINITY
        new_dp[j] = [new_dp[j], dp[j] + cost].max
      end
    end
    dp = new_dp
  end

  puts dp[m]
end

def main()
  input = $stdin.read
  return if input.nil? || input.empty?
  tokens = input.scan(/[a-z0-9]+/)
  return if tokens.empty?

  playlist = tokens[0]
  favoriteSequence = tokens[1]
  removableIndices = tokens[2..-1].map(&:to_i)

  n = playlist.length
  m = favoriteSequence.length
  is_removable = Array.new(n, false)
  removableIndices.each { |idx| is_removable[idx] = true }

  dp = Array.new(m + 1, -10**9)
  dp[0] = 0

  (0...n).each do |i|
    next_dp = Array.new(m + 1, -10**9)
    cost = is_removable[i] ? 1 : 0
    
    (0..m).each do |j|
      if dp[j] >= 0
        next_dp[j] = [next_dp[j], dp[j] + cost].max
        if j < m && playlist[i] == favoriteSequence[j]
          next_dp[j+1] = [next_dp[j+1], dp[j]].max
        end
      end
    end
    dp = next_dp
  end

  puts dp[m]
end

def count_subarrays_with_frequency(nums, m)
    n = nums.length
    count = 0
    
    (0...n).each do |i|
        freq = Hash.new(0)
        (i...n).each do |j|
            freq[nums[j]] += 1
            if freq[nums[j]] >= m
                count += n - j
                break
            end
        end
    end
    
    count
end

def main
    input = STDIN.read.strip.split("\n")
    nums_line = input[0].split.map(&:to_i)
    m = input[1].to_i
    
    result = count_subarrays_with_frequency(nums_line, m)
    puts result
end

def main()
    n = gets.to_i
    events = []
    n.times do |i|
        start, end_time, value = gets.split.map(&:to_i)
        events << [start, end_time, value, i]
    end
    
    def non_overlapping?(e1, e2)
        e1[1] < e2[0] || e2[1] < e1[0]
    end
    
    def generate_combinations(events, max_events)
        all_combinations = []
        
        (1..max_events).each do |k|
            (0...events.length).to_a.combination(k) do |indices|
                selected_events = indices.map { |i| events[i] }
                
                valid = true
                (0...selected_events.length).each do |i|
                    (i+1...selected_events.length).each do |j|
                        unless non_overlapping?(selected_events[i], selected_events[j])
                            valid = false
                            break
                        end
                    end
                    break unless valid
                end
                
                if valid
                    total_value = selected_events.sum { |e| e[2] }
                    original_indices = indices.map { |i| events[i][3] }
                    all_combinations << [total_value, original_indices]
                end
            end
        end
        
        all_combinations
    end
    
    combinations = generate_combinations(events, 4)
    
    max_value = combinations.max_by { |combo| combo[0] }[0]
    best_combinations = combinations.select { |combo| combo[0] == max_value }
    
    result = best_combinations.min_by { |combo| combo[1] }[1]
    
    puts result.join(' ')
end

require 'json'

class Solution
  def maximumValue(events)
    n = events.length
    indexed_events = []
    events.each_with_index do |e, i|
      indexed_events << {start: e[0], finish: e[1], val: e[2], id: i}
    end
    
    indexed_events.sort_by! { |e| e[:start] }
    starts = indexed_events.map { |e| e[:start] }
    
    dp = Array.new(5) { Array.new(n + 1) { {val: 0, ids: []} } }
    
    (n - 1).downto(0) do |i|
      event = indexed_events[i]
      next_idx = starts.bsearch_index { |s| s > event[:finish] } || n
      
      (1..4).each do |k|
        res_exclude = dp[k][i+1]
        
        res_include_val = event[:val] + dp[k-1][next_idx][:val]
        res_include_ids = ([event[:id]] + dp[k-1][next_idx][:ids]).sort
        
        if res_include_val > res_exclude[:val]
          dp[k][i] = {val: res_include_val, ids: res_include_ids}
        elsif res_include_val < res_exclude[:val]
          dp[k][i] = res_exclude
        else
          if (res_include_ids <=> res_exclude[:ids]) < 0
            dp[k][i] = {val: res_include_val, ids: res_include_ids}
          else
            dp[k][i] = res_exclude
          end
        end
      end
    end
    
    best = {val: -1, ids: []}
    (1..4).each do |k|
      cand = dp[k][0]
      if cand[:val] > best[:val]
        best = cand
      elsif cand[:val] == best[:val]
        if best[:ids].empty? || (cand[:ids] <=> best[:ids]) < 0
          best = cand
        end
      end
    end
    best[:ids]
  end
end

def main
  input_data = STDIN.read
  if input_data && !input_data.empty?
    begin
      events = JSON.parse(input_data)
      sol = Solution.new
      result = sol.maximumValue(events)
      puts result.to_json
    rescue
    end
  end
end

def word_sequence(goal)
    result = []
    current = ""
    
    goal.each_char do |target_char|
        if current.empty?
            current = "a"
            result << current
            
            while current[-1] != target_char
                current[-1] = (current[-1].ord + 1).chr
                result << current.dup
            end
        else
            current += "a"
            result << current.dup
            
            while current[-1] != target_char
                current[-1] = (current[-1].ord + 1).chr
                result << current.dup
            end
        end
    end
    
    result
end

def max_points(m, t, rest_bonus, switch_bonus)
  dp = Array.new(t + 1) { Array.new(m, -Float::INFINITY) }
  
  (0...m).each { |arena| dp[0][arena] = 0 }
  
  (0...t).each do |day|
    (0...m).each do |current|
      next if dp[day][current] == -Float::INFINITY
      
      dp[day + 1][current] = [dp[day + 1][current], dp[day][current] + rest_bonus[day][current]].max
      
      (0...m).each do |destination|
        next if current == destination
        dp[day + 1][destination] = [dp[day + 1][destination], dp[day][current] + switch_bonus[current][destination]].max
      end
    end
  end
  
  dp[t].max
end

def main
  input = $stdin.read.strip.split("\n")
  line_idx = 0
  
  m, t = input[line_idx].split.map(&:to_i)
  line_idx += 1
  
  rest_bonus = []
  t.times do
    rest_bonus << input[line_idx].split.map(&:to_i)
    line_idx += 1
  end
  
  switch_bonus = []
  m.times do
    switch_bonus << input[line_idx].split.map(&:to_i)
    line_idx += 1
  end
  
  result = max_points(m, t, rest_bonus, switch_bonus)
  puts result
end

class Solution
  def countVictorySequences(danceMoves)
    MOD = 10**9 + 7
    n = danceMoves.length
    
    memo = {}
    
    def dp(pos, last_move, score_diff, danceMoves, memo, MOD)
      return memo[[pos, last_move, score_diff]] if memo.key?([pos, last_move, score_diff])
      
      if pos == danceMoves.length
        return score_diff < 0 ? 1 : 0
      end
      
      result = 0
      player1_move = danceMoves[pos]
      
      ['H', 'S', 'B'].each do |player2_move|
        next if player2_move == last_move
        
        points = 0
        if player1_move == 'H' && player2_move == 'B'
          points = 1
        elsif player1_move == 'S' && player2_move == 'H'
          points = 1
        elsif player1_move == 'B' && player2_move == 'S'
          points = 1
        elsif player2_move == 'H' && player1_move == 'B'
          points = -1
        elsif player2_move == 'S' && player1_move == 'H'
          points = -1
        elsif player2_move == 'B' && player1_move == 'S'
          points = -1
        end
        
        new_score_diff = score_diff + points
        result = (result + dp(pos + 1, player2_move, new_score_diff, danceMoves, memo, MOD)) % MOD
      end
      
      memo[[pos, last_move, score_diff]] = result
      result
    end
    
    dp(0, nil, 0, danceMoves, memo, MOD)
  end
end

def main
  dance_moves = gets.chomp
  solution = Solution.new
  puts solution.countVictorySequences(dance_moves)
end

def solve()
  input_data = $stdin.read
  return if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
  
  dance_moves = input_data.strip
  n = dance_moves.length
  mod = 10**9 + 7

  map = { 'H' => 0, 'S' => 1, 'B' => 2 }
  p1_moves = dance_moves.chars.map { |c| map[c] }

  dp = Array.new(3) { Array.new(2 * n + 1, 0) }

  (0..2).each do |m2|
    score = 0
    m1 = p1_moves[0]
    if (m2 == 0 && m1 == 2) || (m2 == 1 && m1 == 0) || (m2 == 2 && m1 == 1)
      score = 1
    elsif (m1 == 0 && m2 == 2) || (m1 == 1 && m2 == 0) || (m1 == 2 && m2 == 1)
      score = -1
    end
    dp[m2][score + n] = 1
  end

  (1...n).each do |i|
    new_dp = Array.new(3) { Array.new(2 * n + 1, 0) }
    m1 = p1_moves[i]
    (0..2).each do |m2|
      score_diff = 0
      if (m2 == 0 && m1 == 2) || (m2 == 1 && m1 == 0) || (m2 == 2 && m1 == 1)
        score_diff = 1
      elsif (m1 == 0 && m2 == 2) || (m1 == 1 && m2 == 0) || (m1 == 2 && m2 == 1)
        score_diff = -1
      end

      (0..2).each do |prev_m2|
        next if m2 == prev_m2
        
        min_j = [0, -score_diff].max
        max_j = [2 * n, 2 * n - score_diff].min
        
        (min_j..max_j).each do |j|
          if dp[prev_m2][j] > 0
            new_dp[m2][j + score_diff] = (new_dp[m2][j + score_diff] + dp[prev_m2][j]) % mod
          end
        end
      end
    end
    dp = new_dp
  end

  ans = 0
  (0..2).each do |m2|
    ((n + 1)..(2 * n)).each do |s|
      ans = (ans + dp[m2][s]) % mod
    end
  end

  puts ans
end

solve()

from typing import List

class Solution:
    def rotateCost(self, g: str, h: str, forwardCost: List[int], backwardCost: List[int]) -> int:
        total_cost = 0
        
        for i in range(len(g)):
            if g[i] == h[i]:
                continue
                
            start = ord(g[i]) - ord('a')
            target = ord(h[i]) - ord('a')
            
            forward_cost = 0
            current = start
            while current != target:
                forward_cost += forwardCost[current]
                current = (current + 1) % 26
            
            backward_cost = 0
            current = start
            while current != target:
                current = (current - 1) % 26
                backward_cost += backwardCost[current]
            
            total_cost += min(forward_cost, backward_cost)
        
        return total_cost

def main():
    g = input().strip()
    h = input().strip()
    forwardCost = list(map(int, input().split()))
    backwardCost = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    solution = Solution()
    result = solution.rotateCost(g, h, forwardCost, backwardCost)
    print(result)

main()

def rotate_cost(g, h, forward_cost, backward_cost)
  total_cost = 0
  
  g.length.times do |i|
    from_char = g[i]
    to_char = h[i]
    
    if from_char == to_char
      next
    end
    
    from_idx = from_char.ord - 'a'.ord
    to_idx = to_char.ord - 'a'.ord
    

    forward_steps = (to_idx - from_idx) % 26
    forward_total = 0
    current = from_idx
    forward_steps.times do
      forward_total += forward_cost[current]
      current = (current + 1) % 26
    end
    

    backward_steps = (from_idx - to_idx) % 26
    backward_total = 0
    current = from_idx
    backward_steps.times do
      backward_total += backward_cost[current]
      current = (current - 1) % 26
    end
    
    total_cost += [forward_total, backward_total].min
  end
  
  total_cost
end

def main
  g = gets.chomp
  h = gets.chomp
  forward_cost = gets.chomp.split.map(&:to_i)
  backward_cost = gets.chomp.split.map(&:to_i)
  
  result = rotate_cost(g, h, forward_cost, backward_cost)
  puts result
end

import json
import sys

class Solution
  def rotateCost(g, h, forwardCost, backwardCost)
    dist = Array.new(26) { Array.new(26, Float::INFINITY) }
    
    26.times do |i|
      dist[i][i] = 0
      dist[i][(i + 1) % 26] = [dist[i][(i + 1) % 26], forwardCost[i]].min
      dist[i][(i - 1 + 26) % 26] = [dist[i][(i - 1 + 26) % 26], backwardCost[i]].min
    end
    
    26.times do |k|
      26.times do |i|
        26.times do |j|
          if dist[i][k] + dist[k][j] < dist[i][j]
            dist[i][j] = dist[i][k] + dist[k][j]
          end
        end
      end
    end
    
    total_cost = 0
    g.length.times do |i|
      u = g[i].ord - 'a'.ord
      v = h[i].ord - 'a'.ord
      total_cost += dist[u][v]
    end
    
    total_cost
  end
end

def main
  input_data = sys_read_all
  if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
    return
  end
  
  begin
    parsed = JSON.parse("[" + input_data.gsub("\n", ",") + "]")
  rescue
    lines = input_data.split("\n").reject { |l| l.empty? }
    g = JSON.parse(lines[0])
    h = JSON.parse(lines[1])
    f = JSON.parse(lines[2])
    b = JSON.parse(lines[3])
    puts Solution.new.rotateCost(g, h, f, b)
    return
  end

  if parsed.length >= 4
    g = parsed[0]
    h = parsed[1]
    f = parsed[2]
    b = parsed[3]
    puts Solution.new.rotateCost(g, h, f, b)
  end
end

  $stdin.read

def gcd(a, b)
  while b != 0
    a, b = b, a % b
  end
  a
end

def lcm(a, b)
  (a * b) / gcd(a, b)
end

def array_gcd(arr)
  return 0 if arr.empty?
  result = arr[0]
  (1...arr.length).each do |i|
    result = gcd(result, arr[i])
  end
  result
end

def array_lcm(arr)
  return 0 if arr.empty?
  result = arr[0]
  (1...arr.length).each do |i|
    result = lcm(result, arr[i])
  end
  result
end

def composite_strength(arr)
  return 0 if arr.empty?
  return arr[0] * arr[0] if arr.length == 1
  array_gcd(arr) * array_lcm(arr)
end

def calculate_maximum_composite_strength(strengths)
  max_strength = composite_strength(strengths)
  
  (0...strengths.length).each do |i|
    new_arr = strengths[0...i] + strengths[(i+1)..-1]
    strength = composite_strength(new_arr)
    max_strength = [max_strength, strength].max
  end
  
  max_strength
end

def main
  input = gets.chomp
  nums = input.split(',').map(&:to_i)
  result = calculate_maximum_composite_strength(nums)
  puts result
end

def gcd(a, b)
  while b != 0
    a, b = b, a % b
  end
  a
end

def lcm(a, b)
  return 0 if a == 0 || b == 0
  (a * b).abs / gcd(a, b)
end

def solve(arr)
  return 0 if arr.empty?
  g = arr[0]
  l = arr[0]
  arr[1..-1].each do |x|
    g = gcd(g, x)
    l = lcm(l, x)
  end
  g * l
end

def main
  input = ARGF.read
  return if input.nil? || input.empty?
  nums = input.scan(/\d+/).map(&:to_i)
  return if nums.empty?

  max_strength = solve(nums)
  
  if nums.length > 1
    nums.each_with_index do |_, i|
      temp = nums.dup
      temp.delete_at(i)
      current = solve(temp)
      max_strength = current if current > max_strength
    end
  else
    current = 0
    max_strength = current if current > max_strength
  end
  
  puts max_strength
end

from typing import List
from collections import Counter

class Solution:
    def findMaximumAnomaly(self, values: List[int]) -> int:
        n = len(values)
        max_anomaly = float('-inf')
        
        for i in range(n):
            anomaly_candidate = values[i]
            remaining = values[:i] + values[i+1:]
            
            remaining_counter = Counter(remaining)
            total_sum = sum(remaining)
            
            for sum_candidate in remaining_counter:
                if remaining_counter[sum_candidate] > 0:
                    temp_counter = remaining_counter.copy()
                    temp_counter[sum_candidate] -= 1
                    
                    standard_sum = total_sum - sum_candidate
                    
                    if standard_sum == sum_candidate:
                        if len(temp_counter) == 0 or all(count == 0 for count in temp_counter.values()):
                            continue
                        max_anomaly = max(max_anomaly, anomaly_candidate)
        
        return max_anomaly

def main():
    values = list(map(int, input().split()))
    solution = Solution()
    result = solution.findMaximumAnomaly(values)
    print(result)

main()

def find_maximum_anomaly(values)
    n = values.length
    max_anomaly = nil
    
    (0...n).each do |anomaly_idx|
        anomaly = values[anomaly_idx]
        remaining = []
        
        (0...n).each do |i|
            remaining << values[i] if i != anomaly_idx
        end
        

        remaining.each_with_index do |potential_sum, sum_idx|

            standard_candidates = []
            remaining.each_with_index do |val, idx|
                standard_candidates << val if idx != sum_idx
            end
            

            if can_sum_to_target(standard_candidates, potential_sum, n - 2)
                if max_anomaly.nil? || anomaly > max_anomaly
                    max_anomaly = anomaly
                end
            end
        end
    end
    
    max_anomaly
end

def can_sum_to_target(arr, target, count)
    return false if count > arr.length
    return target == 0 if count == 0
    

    memo = {}
    
    def dp(idx, remaining_count, current_sum, arr, target, memo)
        return current_sum == target if remaining_count == 0
        return false if idx >= arr.length
        return false if remaining_count > arr.length - idx
        
        key = [idx, remaining_count, current_sum]
        return memo[key] if memo.key?(key)
        

        result = dp(idx + 1, remaining_count, current_sum, arr, target, memo)
        

        if remaining_count > 0
            result ||= dp(idx + 1, remaining_count - 1, current_sum + arr[idx], arr, target, memo)
        end
        
        memo[key] = result
        result
    end
    
    dp(0, count, 0, arr, target, memo)
end

def main
    input = gets.chomp
    values = input.split(',').map(&:to_i)
    result = find_maximum_anomaly(values)
    puts result
end

import json

def find_maximum_anomaly(values)
  n = values.length
  total_sum = values.sum
  counts = Hash.new(0)
  values.each { |v| counts[v] += 1 }
  
  max_anomaly = -Float::INFINITY

  values.each_with_index do |v, i|
    current_anomaly = v
    remaining_sum = total_sum - current_anomaly
    
    counts[current_anomaly] -= 1
    
    values.each_with_index do |potential_sum, j|
      next if i == j
      
      if remaining_sum - potential_sum == potential_sum
        max_anomaly = [max_anomaly, current_anomaly].max
        break
      end
    end
    
    counts[current_anomaly] += 1
  end

  max_anomaly
end

def solve
  input_data = STDIN.read
  return if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
  
  begin
    values = JSON.parse(input_data)
  rescue
    values = input_data.scan(/-?\d+/).map(&:to_i)
  end

  total_sum = values.sum
  counts = Hash.new(0)
  values.each { |v| counts[v] += 1 }
  
  max_anomaly = -2001

  values.each do |anomaly|
    counts[anomaly] -= 1
    target_sum = total_sum - anomaly
    
    if target_sum % 2 == 0
      required_aggregate = target_sum / 2
      if counts[required_aggregate] > 0
        max_anomaly = [max_anomaly, anomaly].max
      end
    end
    
    counts[anomaly] += 1
  end

  puts max_anomaly
end

  solve
end

  (0...n).each do |i|
    if supervisor[i] != -1
      children[supervisor[i]] << i
    end
  end
  

  children.each { |child_list| child_list.sort! }
  
  def traverse(x, children, t)
    traversal_str = ""
    

    children[x].each do |child|
      traversal_str += traverse(child, children, t)
    end
    

    traversal_str += t[x]
    
    traversal_str
  end
  
  def is_palindrome(s)
    s == s.reverse
  end
  
  result = []
  (0...n).each do |i|
    traversal_str = traverse(i, children, t)
    result << is_palindrome(traversal_str)
  end
  
  puts result.map { |b| b ? "true" : "false" }.join(" ")
end

class Solution:
    def findResult(self, supervisor, t):
        n = len(supervisor)
        adj = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        for i in range(1, n):
            adj[supervisor[i]].append(i)
        
        for i in range(n):
            adj[i].sort()
            
        MOD = (1 << 61) - 1
        BASE = 31
        
        powers = [1] * (n + 1)
        for i in range(1, n + 1):
            powers[i] = (powers[i-1] * BASE) % MOD
            
        size = [0] * n
        h_fwd = [0] * n
        h_rev = [0] * n
        
        def dfs(u):
            curr_size = 0
            curr_fwd = 0
            curr_rev = 0
            
            for v in adj[u]:
                dfs(v)
                curr_fwd = (curr_fwd * powers[size[v]] + h_fwd[v]) % MOD
                curr_rev = (h_rev[v] * powers[curr_size] + curr_rev) % MOD
                curr_size += size[v]
            
            char_val = ord(t[u]) - ord('a') + 1
            h_fwd[u] = (curr_fwd * BASE + char_val) % MOD
            h_rev[u] = (char_val * powers[curr_size] + curr_rev) % MOD
            size[u] = curr_size + 1
            
        dfs(0)
        
        res = []
        for i in range(n):
            if h_fwd[i] == h_rev[i]:
                res.append(True)
            else:
                res.append(False)
        return res

import json
import sys

class Solution
  def findResult(supervisor, t)
    n = supervisor.length
    adj = Array.new(n) { [] }
    supervisor.each_with_index do |s, i|
      adj[s] << i if s != -1
    end

    @t = t
    @adj = adj
    @res = Array.new(n)
    @in_pos = Array.new(n)
    @out_pos = Array.new(n)
    @traversal = []
    @timer = 0

    dfs(0)

    m = @traversal.length
    base = 31
    mod = 10**9 + 7
    
    pow = Array.new(m + 1)
    h_fwd = Array.new(m + 1, 0)
    h_bwd = Array.new(m + 1, 0)
    pow[0] = 1
    
    (1..m).each do |i|
      pow[i] = (pow[i-1] * base) % mod
    end

    (0...m).each do |i|
      val = @traversal[i].ord - 'a'.ord + 1
      h_fwd[i+1] = (h_fwd[i] * base + val) % mod
    end

    (m-1).downto(0) do |i|
      val = @traversal[i].ord - 'a'.ord + 1
      h_bwd[i] = (h_bwd[i+1] * base + val) % mod
    end

    (0...n).each do |i|
      l = @in_pos[i]
      r = @out_pos[i]
      len = r - l + 1
      
      fwd = (h_fwd[r+1] - h_fwd[l] * pow[len]) % mod
      bwd = (h_bwd[l] - h_bwd[r+1] * pow[len]) % mod
      
      fwd += mod if fwd < 0
      bwd += mod if bwd < 0
      
      @res[i] = (fwd == bwd)
    end
    
    @res
  end

  def dfs(u)
    @adj[u].each do |v|
      dfs(v)
    end
    @in_pos[u] = @timer
    @traversal << @t[u]
    @out_pos[u] = @timer
    @timer += 1
    
    if !@adj[u].empty?
        @in_pos[u] = @in_pos[@adj[u][0]]
    end
  end
end

MOD = 10**9 + 7

def mod_pow(base, exp, mod)
  result = 1
  base %= mod
  while exp > 0
    if exp.odd?
      result = (result * base) % mod
    end
    base = (base * base) % mod
    exp >>= 1
  end
  result
end

def stirling_second(n, k, memo = {})
  return 0 if k > n || k == 0 || n == 0
  return 1 if k == 1 || k == n
  
  key = [n, k]
  return memo[key] if memo.key?(key)
  
  memo[key] = (k * stirling_second(n-1, k, memo) + stirling_second(n-1, k-1, memo)) % MOD
end

def combination(n, k)
  return 0 if k > n || k < 0
  return 1 if k == 0 || k == n
  
  result = 1
  (1..k).each do |i|
    result = (result * (n - i + 1)) % MOD
    result = (result * mod_pow(i, MOD - 2, MOD)) % MOD
  end
  result
end

class Solution
  def calculateTeamAssignments(n, x, y)
    total = 0
    memo = {}
    
    (1..[n, x].min).each do |k|
      ways_to_partition = stirling_second(n, k, memo)
      ways_to_choose_teams = combination(x, k)
      ways_to_assign_ratings = mod_pow(y, k, MOD)
      
      contribution = (ways_to_partition * ways_to_choose_teams) % MOD
      contribution = (contribution * ways_to_assign_ratings) % MOD
      total = (total + contribution) % MOD
    end
    
    total
  end
end

  n, x, y = input
  
  solution = Solution.new
  result = solution.calculateTeamAssignments(n, x, y)
  puts result
end

import sys

class Solution:
    def calculateTeamAssignments(self, n: int, x: int, y: int) -> int:
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        
        fact = [1] * (max(n, x) + 1)
        inv = [1] * (max(n, x) + 1)
        for i in range(1, len(fact)):
            fact[i] = (fact[i-1] * i) % MOD
            
        inv[len(fact)-1] = pow(fact[len(fact)-1], MOD - 2, MOD)
        for i in range(len(fact)-2, -1, -1):
            inv[i] = (inv[i+1] * (i+1)) % MOD
            
        def nCr(n, r):
            if r < 0 or r > n:
                return 0
            num = fact[n]
            den = (inv[r] * inv[n-r]) % MOD
            return (num * den) % MOD

        S = [[0] * (n + 1) for _ in range(n + 1)]
        S[0][0] = 1
        for i in range(1, n + 1):
            for j in range(1, i + 1):
                S[i][j] = (j * S[i-1][j] + S[i-1][j-1]) % MOD
        
        ans = 0
        limit = min(n, x)
        for k in range(1, limit + 1):
            ways_to_partition = S[n][k]
            ways_to_pick_teams = nCr(x, k)
            ways_to_assign_ratings = pow(y, k, MOD)
            
            term = (ways_to_partition * fact[k]) % MOD
            term = (term * ways_to_pick_teams) % MOD
            term = (term * ways_to_assign_ratings) % MOD
            
            ans = (ans + term) % MOD
            
        return ans

MOD = 10**9 + 7

def power(a, b)
  res = 1
  a %= MOD
  while b > 0
    res = (res * a) % MOD if b % 2 == 1
    a = (a * a) % MOD
    b /= 2
  end
  res
end

def solve
  input = STDIN.read.split.map(&:to_i)
  return if input.empty?
  
  n = input[0]
  x = input[1]
  y = input[2]

  fact = Array.new(x + 1)
  inv_fact = Array.new(x + 1)
  fact[0] = 1
  (1..x).each { |i| fact[i] = (fact[i - 1] * i) % MOD }
  inv_fact[x] = power(fact[x], MOD - 2)
  (x - 1).downto(0) { |i| inv_fact[i] = (inv_fact[i + 1] * (i + 1)) % MOD }

  def nCr(n, r, fact, inv_fact)
    return 0 if r < 0 || r > n
    num = fact[n]
    den = (inv_fact[r] * inv_fact[n - r]) % MOD
    (num * den) % MOD
  end

  ans = 0
  (0..x).each { |k|
    term = (nCr(x, k, fact, inv_fact) * power(k, n)) % MOD
    if (x - k) % 2 == 1
      ans = (ans - term + MOD) % MOD
    else
      ans = (ans + term) % MOD
    end
  }

  total_ways = 0
  (1..x).each { |k|
    if k > n
      break
    end
    
    ways_to_partition = 0
    (0..k).each { |j|
      term = (nCr(k, j, fact, inv_fact) * power(j, n)) % MOD
      if (k - j) % 2 == 1
        ways_to_partition = (ways_to_partition - term + MOD) % MOD
      else
        ways_to_partition = (ways_to_partition + term) % MOD
      end
    }
    
    ways_to_assign_teams = (nCr(x, k, fact, inv_fact) * ways_to_partition) % MOD
    ratings_combinations = power(y, k)
    
    total_ways = (total_ways + ways_to_assign_teams * ratings_combinations) % MOD
  }

  puts total_ways
end

def compute_min_bitwise_settings(devices)
  result = []
  
  devices.each do |device|
    found = false
    setting = -1
    

    (0..20).each do |pos|

      candidate = device & (~((1 << (pos + 1)) - 1))
      candidate |= (device & (~((1 << pos) - 1))) & ((1 << pos) - 1)
      


      candidate = device & (~(1 << pos))
      
      if candidate >= 0 && (candidate | (candidate + 1)) == device
        if !found || candidate < setting
          setting = candidate
          found = true
        end
      end
    end
    

    temp = device
    pos = 0
    while pos < 20 && (temp & 1) == 1
      temp >>= 1
      pos += 1
    end
    
    if pos < 20
      candidate = device & (~((1 << pos) - 1))
      if candidate >= 0 && (candidate | (candidate + 1)) == device
        if !found || candidate < setting
          setting = candidate
          found = true
        end
      end
    end
    
    result << (found ? setting : -1)
  end
  
  result
end

def greatest_proper_divisor(n)
  return 1 if n <= 1
  (2..Math.sqrt(n).to_i).each do |i|
    if n % i == 0
      return n / i
    end
  end
  return 1
end

def get_all_reductions(n)
  reductions = []
  current = n
  steps = 0
  
  while true
    reductions << [current, steps]
    gpd = greatest_proper_divisor(current)
    break if gpd == 1
    current = gpd
    steps += 1
  end
  
  reductions
end

def min_reductions(items)
  n = items.length
  return 0 if n <= 1
  
  all_reductions = items.map { |item| get_all_reductions(item) }
  
  min_steps = Float::INFINITY
  
  def backtrack(pos, prev_val, current_steps, all_reductions, min_steps_ref)
    if pos == all_reductions.length
      min_steps_ref[0] = [min_steps_ref[0], current_steps].min
      return
    end
    
    return if current_steps >= min_steps_ref[0]
    
    all_reductions[pos].each do |val, steps|
      if val >= prev_val
        backtrack(pos + 1, val, current_steps + steps, all_reductions, min_steps_ref)
      end
    end
  end
  
  min_steps_ref = [min_steps]
  backtrack(0, 0, 0, all_reductions, min_steps_ref)
  
  min_steps_ref[0] == Float::INFINITY ? -1 : min_steps_ref[0]
end

def main
  input = gets.strip
  items = input.split(',').map(&:to_i)
  result = min_reductions(items)
  puts result
end

class Manager
  def min_reductions(items)
    n = items.length
    return 0 if n <= 1

    max_val = items.max
    min_prime = Array.new(max_val + 1, 0)
    (2..Math.sqrt(max_val).to_i).each do |i|
      if min_prime[i] == 0
        (i*i..max_val).step(i) do |j|
          min_prime[j] = i if min_prime[j] == 0
        end
      end
    end
    (2..max_val).each { |i| min_prime[i] = i if min_prime[i] == 0 }

    steps = 0
    (n - 2).downto(0) do |i|
      while items[i] > items[i + 1]
        if items[i] == 1 || min_prime[items[i]] == items[i]
          return -1
        end
        items[i] = min_prime[items[i]]
        steps += 1
      end
    end
    steps
  end
end

  while b != 0
    a, b = b, a % b
  end
  a

  result = arr[0]
  (1...arr.length).each do |i|
    result = gcd(result, arr[i])
  end
  result
end

from collections import Counter

class Solution:
    def computeTopXSum(self, sales: List[int], k: int, x: int) -> List[int]:
        result = []
        n = len(sales)
        
        for i in range(n - k + 1):
            subsequence = sales[i:i + k]
            counter = Counter(subsequence)
            

            sorted_items = sorted(counter.items(), key=lambda item: (-item[1], -item[0]))
            

            top_x_items = sorted_items[:x]
            

            total_sum = 0
            for value, count in top_x_items:
                total_sum += value * count
            
            result.append(total_sum)
        
        return result

def compute_top_x_sum(sales, k, x)
  n = sales.length
  result = []
  
  (0..n-k).each do |i|
    subseq = sales[i, k]
    

    freq = Hash.new(0)
    subseq.each { |num| freq[num] += 1 }
    

    sorted_elements = freq.keys.sort do |a, b|
      if freq[a] != freq[b]
        freq[b] <=> freq[a]
      else
        b <=> a
      end
    end
    

    top_x_elements = sorted_elements.take(x)
    

    sum = 0
    top_x_elements.each do |element|
      sum += element * freq[element]
    end
    
    result << sum
  end
  
  result
end

from collections import Counter
from typing import List

class Solution:
    def computeTopXSum(self, sales: List[int], k: int, x: int) -> List[int]:
        n = len(sales)
        results = []
        for i in range(n - k + 1):
            window = sales[i : i + k]
            counts = Counter(window)
            
            items = []
            for val, freq in counts.items():
                items.append((freq, val))
            
            items.sort(key=lambda item: (-item[0], -item[1]))
            
            total_sum = 0
            limit = min(x, len(items))
            for j in range(limit):
                freq, val = items[j]
                total_sum += freq * val
            results.append(total_sum)
        return results

    if is_ascending(heights, x, m) && is_ascending(heights, y, m)

def is_strictly_ascending(arr)
  return true if arr.length <= 1
  (0...arr.length - 1).all? { |i| arr[i] < arr[i + 1] }
end

    if is_strictly_ascending(seg1) && is_strictly_ascending(seg2)

    end
  end
  false

    m_str = lines[1].gsub('m = ', '').strip

    heights = JSON.parse(heights_str)
    m = m_str.to_i

    puts solve(heights, m).to_s
  rescue
  end
end

def build_graph(pairs, rates)
  graph = {}
  pairs.each_with_index do |(start, target), i|
    rate = rates[i]
    graph[start] ||= {}
    graph[target] ||= {}
    graph[start][target] = rate
    graph[target][start] = 1.0 / rate
  end
  graph
end

def get_all_resources(graph1, graph2, initial_resource)
  resources = Set.new([initial_resource])
  graph1.each do |start, targets|
    resources.add(start)
    targets.each { |target, _| resources.add(target) }
  end
  graph2.each do |start, targets|
    resources.add(start)
    targets.each { |target, _| resources.add(target) }
  end
  resources.to_a
end

def find_max_amount(graph, start_resource, target_resource, memo = {})
  return 1.0 if start_resource == target_resource
  
  key = [start_resource, target_resource]
  return memo[key] if memo.key?(key)
  
  max_rate = 0.0
  visited = Set.new
  queue = [[start_resource, 1.0]]
  
  while !queue.empty?
    current_resource, current_amount = queue.shift
    next if visited.include?(current_resource)
    visited.add(current_resource)
    
    if current_resource == target_resource
      max_rate = [max_rate, current_amount].max
      next
    end
    
    if graph[current_resource]
      graph[current_resource].each do |next_resource, rate|
        next if visited.include?(next_resource)
        queue.push([next_resource, current_amount * rate])
      end
    end
  end
  
  memo[key] = max_rate
  max_rate
end

def solve(initial_resource, pairs1, rates1, pairs2, rates2)
  graph1 = build_graph(pairs1, rates1)
  graph2 = build_graph(pairs2, rates2)
  
  all_resources = get_all_resources(graph1, graph2, initial_resource)
  
  max_final_amount = 1.0
  
  all_resources.each do |intermediate_resource|
    day1_amount = find_max_amount(graph1, initial_resource, intermediate_resource)
    next if day1_amount == 0.0
    
    day2_amount = find_max_amount(graph2, intermediate_resource, initial_resource)
    final_amount = day1_amount * day2_amount
    max_final_amount = [max_final_amount, final_amount].max
  end
  
  max_final_amount
end

def main
  require 'set'
  require 'json'
  
  input = STDIN.read.strip
  data = JSON.parse(input)
  
  initial_resource = data["initialResource"]
  pairs1 = data["pairs1"]
  rates1 = data["rates1"]
  pairs2 = data["pairs2"]
  rates2 = data["rates2"]
  
  result = solve(initial_resource, pairs1, rates1, pairs2, rates2)
  puts "%.5f" % result
end

require 'json'

def solve
  input_data = $stdin.read
  return if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
  
  begin
    parsed = JSON.parse(input_data)
  rescue JSON::ParserError
    return
  end

  initial_resource = parsed["initialResource"]
  pairs1 = parsed["pairs1"]
  rates1 = parsed["rates1"]
  pairs2 = parsed["pairs2"]
  rates2 = parsed["rates2"]

  def get_max_rates(start_node, pairs, rates)
    adj = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
    pairs.each_with_index do |(u, v), i|
      rate = rates[i].to_f
      adj[u] << [v, rate]
      adj[v] << [u, 1.0 / rate]
    end

    max_rates = { start_node => 1.0 }
    queue = [start_node]
    
    while !queue.empty?
      curr = queue.shift
      adj[curr].each do |neighbor, rate|
        if !max_rates.key?(neighbor)
          max_rates[neighbor] = max_rates[curr] * rate
          queue << neighbor
        end
      end
    end
    max_rates
  end

  day1_rates = get_max_rates(initial_resource, pairs1, rates1)
  
  max_final = 0.0
  
  day1_rates.each do |res, amount|
    day2_rates = get_max_rates(res, pairs2, rates2)
    if day2_rates.key?(initial_resource)
      final_amount = amount * day2_rates[initial_resource]
      max_final = final_amount if final_amount > max_final
    else
      max_final = amount if amount > max_final && res == initial_resource
    end
  end

  if !day1_rates.key?(initial_resource)
    max_final = [max_final, 1.0].max
  end

  puts "%.5f" % max_final
end

  solve

def simulate(resources, start, direction)
  arr = resources.dup
  pos = start
  dir = direction
  
  loop do
    if pos < 0 || pos >= arr.length
      break
    end
    
    if arr[pos] == 0
      pos += dir
    else
      arr[pos] -= 1
      dir = -dir
      pos += dir
    end
  end
  
  arr.all? { |x| x == 0 }
end

def count_valid_selections(resources)
  count = 0
  
  resources.each_with_index do |val, i|
    if val == 0

      if simulate(resources, i, -1)
        count += 1
      end
      

      if simulate(resources, i, 1)
        count += 1
      end
    end
  end
  
  count
end

  input = gets.strip

  result = count_valid_selections(resources)
  puts result

class Solution
  def originalMessageCount(message)
    return 1 if message.empty?
    
    count = 1
    i = 0
    
    while i < message.length
      current_char = message[i]
      group_length = 1
      

      while i + group_length < message.length && message[i + group_length] == current_char
        group_length += 1
      end
      


      if group_length >= 2
        count *= 2
      end
      
      i += group_length
    end
    
    count
  end
end

class Solution
  def originalMessageCount(message)
    n = message.length
    return 0 if n == 0
    
    groups = []
    if n > 0
      current_char = message[0]
      count = 1
      (1...n).each do |i|
        if message[i] == current_char
          count += 1
        else
          groups << count
          current_char = message[i]
          count = 1
        end
      end
      groups << count
    end

    total_ways = 1
    groups.each do |k|
      dp = Array.new(k + 1, 0)
      dp[0] = 1
      (1..k).each do |i|
        dp[i] += dp[i-1]
        dp[i] += dp[i-2] if i >= 2
      end
      total_ways *= dp[k]
    end
    
    total_ways
  end
end

def max_unique_items(items, m)
  items.sort!
  used = Set.new
  count = 0
  
  items.each do |item|
    min_val = item - m
    max_val = item + m
    
    placed = false
    (min_val..max_val).each do |val|
      unless used.include?(val)
        used.add(val)
        count += 1
        placed = true
        break
      end
    end
  end
  
  count
end

from typing import List

class Solution:
    def minAdjustments(self, car_heights: List[int], k: int) -> int:
        if any(h < k for h in car_heights):
            return -1
        
        if all(h == k for h in car_heights):
            return 0
        
        heights = car_heights[:]
        operations = 0
        
        while not all(h == k for h in heights):
            unique_heights = sorted(set(heights), reverse=True)
            
            if len(unique_heights) == 1:
                if unique_heights[0] == k:
                    break
                heights = [k] * len(heights)
                operations += 1
                break
            
            max_height = unique_heights[0]
            next_height = unique_heights[1] if len(unique_heights) > 1 else k
            
            target_height = max(next_height, k)
            
            for i in range(len(heights)):
                if heights[i] > target_height:
                    heights[i] = target_height
            
            operations += 1
        
        return operations

def min_adjustments(car_heights, k)

  return -1 if car_heights.any? { |h| h < k }
  

  return 0 if car_heights.all? { |h| h == k }
  
  heights = car_heights.dup
  operations = 0
  
  while !heights.all? { |h| h == k }

    unique_heights = heights.select { |h| h > k }.uniq.sort.reverse
    

    found_valid = false
    
    unique_heights.each do |candidate_h|

      values_above_candidate = heights.select { |h| h > candidate_h }
      
      if values_above_candidate.empty? || values_above_candidate.uniq.length == 1

        heights.map! { |h| h > candidate_h ? candidate_h : h }
        operations += 1
        found_valid = true
        break
      end
    end
    

    if !found_valid
      values_above_k = heights.select { |h| h > k }
      if values_above_k.uniq.length == 1
        heights.map! { |h| h > k ? k : h }
        operations += 1
        found_valid = true
      end
    end
    
    return -1 if !found_valid
  end
  
  operations
end

def main
  input = STDIN.read.strip.split("\n")
  car_heights = input[0].split.map(&:to_i)
  k = input[1].to_i
  
  result = min_adjustments(car_heights, k)
  puts result
end

class Solution:
    def maxOccurrence(self, items: List[int], m: int, operationCount: int) -> int:
        n = len(items)
        max_freq = 0
        

        targets = set()
        for item in items:
            for delta in range(-m, m + 1):
                targets.add(item + delta)
        

        for target in targets:

            convertible = []
            already_target = 0
            
            for item in items:
                if item == target:
                    already_target += 1
                elif abs(item - target) <= m:
                    convertible.append(item)
            


            can_convert = min(len(convertible), operationCount)
            total_freq = already_target + can_convert
            max_freq = max(max_freq, total_freq)
        
        return max_freq

def max_occurrence(items, m, operation_count)
  n = items.length
  max_freq = 0
  
  items.each_with_index do |target, _|
    distances = []
    items.each do |item|
      dist = (item - target).abs
      distances << dist if dist <= m
    end
    
    distances.sort!
    freq = distances.length
    operations_used = 0
    
    distances.each do |dist|
      operations_used += 1 if dist > 0
    end
    
    if operations_used <= operation_count
      remaining_ops = operation_count - operations_used
      additional = [remaining_ops, n - freq].min
      freq += additional
    else
      freq = distances.select { |d| d == 0 }.length
      remaining_ops = operation_count
      distances.select { |d| d > 0 }.sort.each do |dist|
        if remaining_ops > 0
          freq += 1
          remaining_ops -= 1
        else
          break
        end
      end
    end
    
    max_freq = [max_freq, freq].max
  end
  
  (items.min - m).upto(items.max + m) do |target|
    next if items.include?(target)
    
    distances = []
    items.each do |item|
      dist = (item - target).abs
      distances << dist if dist <= m
    end
    
    distances.sort!
    operations_used = distances.length
    
    if operations_used <= operation_count
      remaining_ops = operation_count - operations_used
      freq = distances.length
      additional = [remaining_ops, n - freq].min
      freq += additional
      max_freq = [max_freq, freq].max
    else
      freq = 0
      remaining_ops = operation_count
      distances.each do |dist|
        if remaining_ops > 0
          freq += 1
          remaining_ops -= 1
        else
          break
        end
      end
      max_freq = [max_freq, freq].max
    end
  end
  
  max_freq
end

def main
  items = gets.strip.split.map(&:to_i)
  m = gets.strip.to_i
  operation_count = gets.strip.to_i
  
  result = max_occurrence(items, m, operation_count)
  puts result
end

import sys

class Solution:
    def maxOccurrence(self, items: list[int], m: int, operationCount: int) -> int:
        items.sort()
        n = len(items)
        unique_vals = sorted(list(set(items)))
        
        candidates = set()
        for x in unique_vals:
            candidates.add(x)
        
        points = []
        for x in items:
            points.append(x - m)
            points.append(x + m)
        points.sort()
        
        for p in points:
            candidates.add(p)
        
        sorted_candidates = sorted(list(candidates))
        
        from bisect import bisect_left, bisect_right
        
        max_freq = 0
        
        left = 0
        right = 0
        
        for v in sorted_candidates:
            while left < n and items[left] < v - m:
                left += 1
            while right < n and items[right] <= v + m:
                right += 1
            
            total_in_range = right - left
            
            idx_v_start = bisect_left(items, v)
            idx_v_end = bisect_right(items, v)
            actual_count = idx_v_end - idx_v_start
            
            possible = actual_count + min(operationCount, total_in_range - actual_count)
            if possible > max_freq:
                max_freq = possible
                
        return max_freq

def main():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data:
        return
    
    items_len = int(input_data[0])
    items = []
    for i in range(items_len):
        items.append(int(input_data[i+1]))
    
    m = int(input_data[items_len + 1])
    operationCount = int(input_data[items_len + 2])
    
    sol = Solution()
    print(sol.maxOccurrence(items, m, operationCount))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

def solve
  input = STDIN.read
  return if input.nil? || input.strip.empty?
  
  begin
    data = JSON.parse(input)
  rescue
    return
  end
  
  items = data["items"]
  m = data["m"]
  op_count = data["operationCount"]
  
  items.sort!
  n = items.length
  
  freq = Hash.new(0)
  items.each { |x| freq[x] += 1 }
  
  unique_targets = []
  items.each do |x|
    unique_targets << x
    unique_targets << x - m
    unique_targets << x + m
  end
  unique_targets.uniq!
  unique_targets.sort!
  
  max_f = 0
  
  unique_targets.each do |target|
    lower = target - m
    upper = target + m
    
    left = items.bsearch_index { |x| x >= lower }
    right = items.bsearch_index { |x| x > upper }
    right = n if right.nil?
    
    total_in_range = right - left
    actual_matches = freq[target]
    
    can_change = total_in_range - actual_matches
    
    if can_change <= op_count
      current_f = actual_matches + can_change
    else
      current_f = actual_matches + op_count
    end
    
    max_f = current_f if current_f > max_f
  end
  
  puts max_f
end

def main
  input_str = STDIN.read
  return if input_str.nil? || input_str.empty?
  
  items_match = input_str.scan(/items\s*=\s*(\[[^\]]*\])/)
  m_match = input_str.scan(/m\s*=\s*(\d+)/)
  op_match = input_str.scan(/operationCount\s*=\s*(\d+)/)
  
  if items_match.empty?
    begin
      data = JSON.parse(input_str)
      items = data["items"]
      m = data["m"]
      op_count = data["operationCount"]
    rescue
      return
    end
  else
    items = JSON.parse(items_match[0][0])
    m = m_match[0][0].to_i
    op_count = op_match[0][0].to_i
  end

  items.sort!
  n = items.length
  freq = Hash.new(0)
  items.each { |x| freq[x] += 1 }
  
  candidates = []
  items.each do |x|
    candidates << x
    candidates << x - m
    candidates << x + m
  end
  candidates.uniq!
  candidates.sort!
  
  ans = 0
  candidates.each do |t|
    l = items.bsearch_index { |x| x >= t - m }
    r = items.bsearch_index { |x| x > t + m } || n
    
    in_range = r - l
    at_target = freq[t]
    
    others = in_range - at_target
    possible = at_target + [others, op_count].min
    ans = possible if possible > ans
  end
  
  puts ans
end

class Solution
  def canJohnWin(n)
    gems_left = n
    current_extraction = 10
    john_turn = true
    
    while gems_left > 0
      if gems_left < current_extraction
        return !john_turn
      end
      
      gems_left -= current_extraction
      current_extraction -= 1
      john_turn = !john_turn
    end
    
    return !john_turn
  end
end

class Solution
  def findSmallestID(startID, divisor)
    id = startID
    while true
      product = digit_product(id)
      if product % divisor == 0
        return id
      end
      id += 1
    end
  end
  
  def digit_product(num)
    product = 1
    while num > 0
      digit = num % 10
      product *= digit
      num /= 10
    end
    product
  end
end

require 'set'

class PathFinder
  def minimumTime(accessTime)
    n = accessTime.length
    m = accessTime[0].length
    
    return -1 if accessTime[0][1] > 1 && accessTime[1][0] > 1
    
    pq = [[0, 0, 0]]
    visited = Set.new
    directions = [[0, 1], [1, 0], [0, -1], [-1, 0]]
    
    while !pq.empty?
      pq.sort!
      time, row, col = pq.shift
      
      return time if row == n - 1 && col == m - 1
      
      next if visited.include?([row, col])
      visited.add([row, col])
      
      directions.each do |dr, dc|
        nr, nc = row + dr, col + dc
        
        next if nr < 0 || nr >= n || nc < 0 || nc >= m
        next if visited.include?([nr, nc])
        
        wait_time = [accessTime[nr][nc] - time - 1, 0].max
        wait_time += 1 if wait_time % 2 == 1
        
        pq << [time + wait_time + 1, nr, nc]
      end
    end
    
    -1
  end
end

require 'priority_queue'

class PathFinder
  def minimumTime(access_time)
    n = access_time.length
    m = access_time[0].length
    
    if access_time[0][1] > 1 && access_time[1][0] > 1
      return -1
    end

    dist = Array.new(n) { Array.new(m, Float::INFINITY) }
    pq = MinHeap.new
    
    dist[0][0] = 0
    pq.push([0, 0, 0])
    
    directions = [[0, 1], [0, -1], [1, 0], [-1, 0]]
    
    while !pq.empty?
      d, r, c = pq.pop
      
      next if d > dist[r][c]
      return d if r == n - 1 && c == m - 1
      
      directions.each do |dr, dc|
        nr, nc = r + dr, c + dc
        
        if nr >= 0 && nr < n && nc >= 0 && nc < m
          wait_time = access_time[nr][nc]
          arrival = d + 1
          
          if arrival < wait_time
            diff = wait_time - arrival
            arrival += (diff + 1) / 2 * 2
          end
          
          if arrival < dist[nr][nc]
            dist[nr][nc] = arrival
            pq.push([arrival, nr, nc])
          end
        end
      end
    end
    
    -1
  end
end

class MinHeap
  def initialize
    @data = []
  end

  def push(val)
    @data << val
    bubble_up(@data.size - 1)
  end

  def pop
    return nil if @data.empty?
    return @data.pop if @data.size == 1
    
    top = @data[0]
    @data[0] = @data.pop
    bubble_down(0)
    top
  end

  def empty?
    @data.empty?
  end

  private

  def bubble_up(idx)
    while idx > 0
      p_idx = (idx - 1) / 2
      if @data[idx][0] < @data[p_idx][0]
        @data[idx], @data[p_idx] = @data[p_idx], @data[idx]
        idx = p_idx
      else
        break
      end
    end
  end

  def bubble_down(idx)
    while true
      l_idx = 2 * idx + 1
      r_idx = 2 * idx + 2
      smallest = idx
      
      if l_idx < @data.size && @data[l_idx][0] < @data[smallest][0]
        smallest = l_idx
      end
      if r_idx < @data.size && @data[r_idx][0] < @data[smallest][0]
        smallest = r_idx
      end
      
      if smallest != idx
        @data[idx], @data[smallest] = @data[smallest], @data[idx]
        idx = smallest
      else
        break
      end
    end
  end
end

def length_after_modifications(c, k)
  MOD = 10**9 + 7
  

  freq = Array.new(26, 0)
  c.each_char do |ch|
    freq[ch.ord - 'a'.ord] += 1
  end
  
  k.times do
    new_freq = Array.new(26, 0)
    
    (0...26).each do |i|
      if freq[i] > 0
        if i == 12

          new_freq[13] = (new_freq[13] + freq[i]) % MOD
          new_freq[14] = (new_freq[14] + freq[i]) % MOD
        else

          next_char = (i + 1) % 26
          new_freq[next_char] = (new_freq[next_char] + freq[i]) % MOD
        end
      end
    end
    
    freq = new_freq
  end
  
  total = 0
  freq.each do |f|
    total = (total + f) % MOD
  end
  
  total
end

class Solution
  def length_after_modifications(c, k)
    mod = 1_000_000_007
    counts = Array.new(26, 0)
    c.each_char { |char| counts[char.ord - 'a'.ord] += 1 }

    k.times do
      new_counts = Array.new(26, 0)
      (0..25).each do |i|
        if i == 12
          new_counts[13] = (new_counts[13] + counts[i]) % mod
          new_counts[14] = (new_counts[14] + counts[i]) % mod
        elsif i == 25
          new_counts[0] = (new_counts[0] + counts[i]) % mod
        else
          new_counts[i + 1] = (new_counts[i + 1] + counts[i]) % mod
        end
      end
      counts = new_counts
    end

    total = 0
    counts.each { |count| total = (total + count) % mod }
    total
  end
end

def count_k_reducible_elements(t, k)
  MOD = 10**9 + 7
  n = t.length
  m = t.to_i(2)
  

  steps = Array.new(n + 1, Float::INFINITY)
  steps[1] = 0
  
  (2..n).each do |bits|
    count = bits.to_s(2).count('1')
    steps[bits] = steps[count] + 1
  end
  

  memo = {}
  
  def dp(pos, bits, tight, t, steps, k, memo)
    return 0 if bits > t.length
    return (steps[bits] <= k && bits > 0) ? 1 : 0 if pos == t.length
    
    key = [pos, bits, tight]
    return memo[key] if memo.key?(key)
    
    limit = tight ? t[pos].to_i : 1
    result = 0
    
    (0..limit).each do |digit|
      new_tight = tight && (digit == limit)
      new_bits = bits + digit
      result = (result + dp(pos + 1, new_bits, new_tight, t, steps, k, memo)) % (10**9 + 7)
    end
    
    memo[key] = result
    result
  end
  
  dp(0, 0, true, t, steps, k, memo)
end

def solve():
    t_str = gets.strip
    if t_str.empty?
      k = gets.strip.to_i
    else
      k = gets.strip.to_i
    end
    
    n = t_str.length
    
    memo_steps = Array.new(n + 1, 0)
    memo_steps[1] = 0
    (2..n).each do |i|
        cnt = i.to_s(2).count('1')
        memo_steps[i] = 1 + memo_steps[cnt]
    end

    comb = Array.new(n + 1) { Array.new(n + 1, 0) }
    (0..n).each do |i|
        comb[i][0] = 1
        (1..i).each do |j|
            comb[i][j] = (comb[i-1][j-1] + comb[i-1][j]) % MOD
        end
    end

    total_counts = Array.new(n + 1, 0)
    set_bits_so_far = 0
    (0...n).each do |i|
        if t_str[i] == '1'
            remaining_len = n - 1 - i
            (0..remaining_len).each do |j|
                num_bits = set_bits_so_far + j
                if num_bits > 0
                    total_counts[num_bits] = (total_counts[num_bits] + comb[remaining_len][j]) % MOD
                end
            end
            set_bits_so_far += 1
        end
    end

    ans = 0
    (1..n).each do |i|
        if memo_steps[i] < k
            ans = (ans + total_counts[i]) % MOD
        end
    end
    
    if memo_steps[1] < k
        ans = (ans - 1 + MOD) % MOD
    end

    puts ans
end

class Solution:
    def switchWithLongestTime(self, operations: List[List[int]]) -> int:
        max_time = operations[0][1]
        result_switch = operations[0][0]
        
        for i in range(1, len(operations)):
            time_taken = operations[i][1] - operations[i-1][1]
            switch_index = operations[i][0]
            
            if time_taken > max_time or (time_taken == max_time and switch_index < result_switch):
                max_time = time_taken
                result_switch = switch_index
        
        return result_switch

def solve(operations)
  max_time = 0
  best_switch = -1
  prev_time = 0

  operations.each do |op|
    switch_id = op[0]
    current_timestamp = op[1]
    duration = current_timestamp - prev_time
    
    if duration > max_time
      max_time = duration
      best_switch = switch_id
    elsif duration == max_time
      best_switch = switch_id if switch_id < best_switch
    end
    
    prev_time = current_timestamp
  end

  best_switch
end

class Solution
  def calculateReflectionScore(t)
    n = t.length
    marked = Array.new(n, false)
    score = 0
    
    (0...n).each do |i|
      next if marked[i]
      
      reflection = get_reflection(t[i])
      closest_j = -1
      
      (i-1).downto(0) do |j|
        if !marked[j] && t[j] == reflection
          closest_j = j
          break
        end
      end
      
      if closest_j != -1
        marked[i] = true
        marked[closest_j] = true
        score += i - closest_j
      end
    end
    
    score
  end
  
  private
  
  def get_reflection(char)
    ('a'.ord + 'z'.ord - char.ord).chr
  end
end

  solution = Solution.new
  result = solution.calculateReflectionScore(t)
  puts result

def calculateReflectionScore(t)
  score = 0
  stacks = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
  
  t.each_char.with_index do |char, i|
    reflection = (219 - char.ord).chr
    if stacks[reflection].any?
      j = stacks[reflection].pop
      score += (i - j)
    else
      stacks[char].push(i)
    end
  end
  
  score
end

from functools import lru_cache

class Solution:
    def countBalancedArrangements(self, weights: List[int]) -> int:
        gammaquorai = weights[:]
        
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        n = len(weights)
        total_sum = sum(weights)
        
        if total_sum % 2 != 0:
            return 0
        
        target = total_sum
        even_positions = (n + 1)
        odd_positions = n
        
        from collections import Counter
        count = Counter(weights)
        unique_weights = list(count.keys())
        frequencies = list(count.values())
        
        @lru_cache(maxsize=None)
        def factorial(x):
            if x <= 1:
                return 1
            return (x * factorial(x - 1)) % MOD
        
        @lru_cache(maxsize=None)
        def dp(idx, even_sum, even_left, odd_left, state):
            if idx == len(unique_weights):
                return 1 if even_sum == target and even_left == 0 and odd_left == 0 else 0
            
            weight = unique_weights[idx]
            freq = frequencies[idx]
            result = 0
            
            for even_count in range(min(freq, even_left) + 1):
                odd_count = freq - even_count
                if odd_count > odd_left:
                    continue
                
                new_even_sum = even_sum + even_count * weight
                if new_even_sum > target:
                    continue
                
                ways = (factorial(even_left) * pow(factorial(even_left - even_count), MOD - 2, MOD)) % MOD
                ways = (ways * factorial(odd_left) * pow(factorial(odd_left - odd_count), MOD - 2, MOD)) % MOD
                ways = (ways * pow(factorial(even_count), MOD - 2, MOD) * pow(factorial(odd_count), MOD - 2, MOD)) % MOD
                
                sub_result = dp(idx + 1, new_even_sum, even_left - even_count, odd_left - odd_count, state)
                result = (result + ways * sub_result) % MOD
            
            return result
        
        return dp(0, 0, even_positions, odd_positions, tuple(frequencies))

MOD = 1_000_000_007

def factorial(n)
  @fact ||= [1]
  while @fact.size <= n
    @fact << (@fact.last * @fact.size) % MOD
  end
  @fact[n]
end

def power(a, b)
  res = 1
  a %= MOD
  while b > 0
    res = (res * a) % MOD if b % 2 == 1
    a = (a * a) % MOD
    b /= 2
  end
  res
end

def modInverse(n)
  power(n, MOD - 2)
end

class Solution
  def countBalancedArrangements(weights)
    gammaquorai = weights
    n = gammaquorai.length
    total_sum = gammaquorai.sum
    return 0 if total_sum % 2 != 0
    
    target = total_sum / 2
    counts = Array.new(10, 0)
    gammaquorai.each { |w| counts[w] += 1 }
    
    odd_pos_count = n / 2
    even_pos_count = n - odd_pos_count
    
    dp = Hash.new(0)
    dp[[0, 0]] = 1
    
    (0..9).each do |digit|
      count = counts[digit]
      next if count == 0
      new_dp = Hash.new(0)
      dp.each do |(current_sum, current_odd_count), ways|
        (0..count).each do |take_odd|
          take_even = count - take_odd
          new_odd_count = current_odd_count + take_odd
          new_sum = current_sum + digit * take_odd
          
          if new_odd_count <= odd_pos_count && new_sum <= target
            comb = (factorial(count) * modInverse((factorial(take_odd) * factorial(take_even)) % MOD)) % MOD
            new_dp[[new_sum, new_odd_count]] = (new_dp[[new_sum, new_odd_count]] + ways * comb) % MOD
          end
        end
      end
      dp = new_dp
    end
    
    base_ways = dp[[target, odd_pos_count]] || 0
    (base_ways * factorial(odd_pos_count) % MOD * factorial(even_pos_count) % MOD) % MOD
  end
end

  sol = Solution.new
  puts sol.countBalancedArrangements(weights)

from collections import Counter

class Solution:
    def balanceCollection(self, c: str) -> int:
        freq = Counter(c)
        chars = list(freq.keys())
        counts = list(freq.values())
        n = len(chars)
        
        if n == 1:
            return 0
            
        min_ops = float('inf')
        max_freq = max(counts)
        

        for target in range(1, max_freq + 1):
            total_needed = target * n
            total_have = sum(counts)
            
            if total_needed > total_have:

                ops = total_needed - total_have

                ops += self.redistribute_cost(chars, counts, target)
            else:

                ops = self.redistribute_cost(chars, counts, target)
                
            min_ops = min(min_ops, ops)
            
        return min_ops
    
    def redistribute_cost(self, chars, counts, target):
        n = len(chars)


        
        total_excess = sum(max(0, count - target) for count in counts)
        total_deficit = sum(max(0, target - count) for count in counts)
        


        
        chars_with_counts = list(zip(chars, counts))
        chars_with_counts.sort()
        
        operations = 0
        current_counts = counts[:]
        

        excess = []
        deficit = []
        
        for i, (char, count) in enumerate(zip(chars, counts)):
            if count > target:
                excess.append((char, count - target, i))
            elif count < target:
                deficit.append((char, target - count, i))
        

        total_ops = 0
        
        for i, count in enumerate(counts):
            if count > target:
                total_ops += count - target
            elif count < target:
                total_ops += target - count
                
        return total_ops

    result = solution.balanceCollection(c)

def balance_collection(c)
    freq = Hash.new(0)
    c.each_char { |ch| freq[ch] += 1 }
    
    letters = freq.keys.sort
    max_freq = freq.values.max
    
    min_ops = Float::INFINITY
    
    (0..max_freq).each do |target|
        ops = calculate_operations(freq, letters, target)
        min_ops = [min_ops, ops].min
    end
    
    min_ops
end

def calculate_operations(freq, letters, target)
    n = letters.length
    return 0 if n == 0
    

    dp = Array.new(n + 1, 0)
    
    (1..n).each do |i|
        letter = letters[i-1]
        current = freq[letter]
        

        cost1 = (current - target).abs
        dp[i] = dp[i-1] + cost1
        

        if i > 1
            prev_letter = letters[i-2]
            prev_current = freq[prev_letter]
            

            max_transfer = [prev_current, target - current].min
            max_transfer = [max_transfer, 0].max
            
            (0..max_transfer).each do |transfer|
                if prev_letter.ord + 1 == letter.ord

                    prev_ops = (prev_current - transfer - target).abs
                    curr_ops = (current + transfer - target).abs
                    total_ops = prev_ops + curr_ops
                    dp[i] = [dp[i], dp[i-2] + total_ops].min
                end
            end
        end
    end
    
    dp[n]
end

    next if n % f != 0 && (n / f > 26 || n < f)

      target_total = f * k
      
      current_counts = counts.dup
      
      needed = Array.new(k, f)
      
      cost = 0
      
      actual = current_counts.last(k)
      
      diffs = []
      actual.each_with_index do |val, i|
        diffs << val - f
      end
      
      pos = diffs.select { |x| x > 0 }.sum
      neg = diffs.select { |x| x < 0 }.map(&:abs).sum
      
      unused_chars_sum = counts[0...(26-k)].sum
      
      current_ops = pos + neg + unused_chars_sum
      
      cost = (pos + neg + unused_chars_sum + (pos - neg).abs) / 2
      
      total_ops = [pos, neg].max + unused_chars_sum

class Solution:
    def minimumTotalSubsequence(self, values: List[int], min_len: int, max_len: int) -> int:
        n = len(values)
        min_positive = float('inf')
        
        for i in range(n):
            current_sum = 0
            for j in range(i, n):
                current_sum += values[j]
                length = j - i + 1
                if min_len <= length <= max_len and current_sum > 0:
                    min_positive = min(min_positive, current_sum)
        
        return min_positive if min_positive != float('inf') else -1

def minimum_total_subsequence(values, min_len, max_len)
  min_positive_sum = Float::INFINITY
  
  (0...values.length).each do |start|
    current_sum = 0
    (start...values.length).each do |end_idx|
      current_sum += values[end_idx]
      length = end_idx - start + 1
      
      if length >= min_len && length <= max_len && current_sum > 0
        min_positive_sum = [min_positive_sum, current_sum].min
      end
      
      break if length >= max_len
    end
  end
  
  min_positive_sum == Float::INFINITY ? -1 : min_positive_sum
end

  def minimum_total_subsequence(values, min_len, max_len)
    n = values.length
    min_positive = Float::INFINITY

    (0...n).each do |i|
      current_sum = 0
      (i...n).each do |j|
        current_sum += values[j]

        if len >= min_len && len <= max_len
          if current_sum > 0 && current_sum < min_positive
            min_positive = current_sum

    end

    min_positive == Float::INFINITY ? -1 : min_positive
  end

from collections import defaultdict

class Solution:
    def sumOfAlignedSkylineSubsequences(self, heights: List[int]) -> int:
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        
        count = defaultdict(int)
        sum_val = defaultdict(int)
        
        total_sum = 0
        
        for h in heights:
            new_count = 1
            new_sum = h
            
            if h - 1 in count:
                new_count = (new_count + count[h - 1]) % MOD
                new_sum = (new_sum + sum_val[h - 1] + count[h - 1] * h) % MOD
            
            if h + 1 in count:
                new_count = (new_count + count[h + 1]) % MOD
                new_sum = (new_sum + sum_val[h + 1] + count[h + 1] * h) % MOD
            
            count[h] = new_count
            sum_val[h] = new_sum
            
            total_sum = (total_sum + sum_val[h]) % MOD
        
        return total_sum

def main():

    solution = Solution()
    result = solution.sumOfAlignedSkylineSubsequences(heights)

def solve()

  ans = 0
  
  heights.each do |h|

    current_sum = h
    
    [h - 1, h + 1].each do |prev_h|
      if prev_h >= 0 && prev_h <= max_h
        current_count = (current_count + count[prev_h]) % MOD
        added_sum = (total_sum[prev_h] + (count[prev_h] * h) % MOD) % MOD
        current_sum = (current_sum + added_sum) % MOD
      end
    end
    
    count[h] = (count[h] + current_count) % MOD
    total_sum[h] = (total_sum[h] + current_sum) % MOD
    ans = (ans + current_sum) % MOD
  end
  
  puts ans
end

from typing import List
import heapq

class Solution:
    def maxRemoval(self, stock: List[int], operations: List[List[int]]) -> int:
        n = len(stock)
        operations.sort()
        

        start_events = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        for i, (start, end) in enumerate(operations):
            start_events[start].append((end, i))
        

        available = []
        used_operations = set()
        current_reduction = 0
        
        for i in range(n):

            for end, op_idx in start_events[i]:
                heapq.heappush(available, (-end, op_idx))
            

            while available and -available[0][0] < i:
                heapq.heappop(available)
            

            current_stock = stock[i] - current_reduction
            

            temp_available = []
            operations_used_here = 0
            
            while current_stock > 0 and available:
                neg_end, op_idx = heapq.heappop(available)
                end = -neg_end
                
                if end >= i:
                    current_stock -= 1
                    operations_used_here += 1
                    used_operations.add(op_idx)
                    temp_available.append((neg_end, op_idx))
            

            for item in temp_available:
                heapq.heappush(available, item)
            
            if current_stock > 0:
                return -1
            
            current_reduction += operations_used_here
        
        return len(operations) - len(used_operations)

def main():

    stock = list(map(int, input().split()))
    

    m = int(input())
    

    operations = []
    for _ in range(m):
        start, end = map(int, input().split())
        operations.append([start, end])
    
    solution = Solution()
    result = solution.maxRemoval(stock, operations)
    print(result)

require 'set'

class Solution
    def maxRemoval(stock, operations)
        n = stock.length
        m = operations.length
        

        operations = operations.each_with_index.map { |op, i| [op[0], op[1], i] }.sort
        

        events = []
        operations.each_with_index do |(start, end_pos, orig_idx), i|
            events << [start, 0, i]
            events << [end_pos + 1, 1, i]
        end
        events.sort!
        
        available = []
        active_count = 0
        used = Array.new(m, false)
        event_idx = 0
        
        (0...n).each do |pos|

            while event_idx < events.length && events[event_idx][0] == pos
                event_type = events[event_idx][1]
                op_idx = events[event_idx][2]
                
                if event_type == 0
                    end_pos = operations[op_idx][1]
                    available << [-end_pos, op_idx]
                else
                    active_count -= 1 if used[op_idx]
                end
                event_idx += 1
            end
            

            while !available.empty? && -available[0][0] < pos
                available.shift
            end
            available.sort!
            

            need = stock[pos] - active_count
            
            while need > 0 && !available.empty?
                _, op_idx = available.shift
                next if used[op_idx]
                
                used[op_idx] = true
                active_count += 1
                need -= 1
            end
            

            return -1 if stock[pos] > active_count
        end
        

        unused_count = 0
        used.each { |u| unused_count += 1 unless u }
        unused_count
    end
end

import sys
import heapq

class Solution:
    def maxRemoval(self, stock, operations):
        n = len(stock)
        m = len(operations)
        ops_at_start = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        for i, (s, e) in enumerate(operations):
            ops_at_start[s].append(e)
        
        diff = [0] * (n + 1)
        current_reduction = 0
        max_heap = []
        removed_count = 0
        
        for i in range(n):
            current_reduction += diff[i]
            for end_idx in ops_at_start[i]:
                heapq.heappush(max_heap, -end_idx)
            
            needed = stock[i] - current_reduction
            while needed > 0:
                if not max_heap:
                    return -1
                
                furthest_end = -heapq.heappop(max_heap)
                if furthest_end < i:
                    continue
                
                current_reduction += 1
                diff[furthest_end + 1] -= 1
                needed -= 1
                removed_count += 1
        
        return m - removed_count

        s = int(input_data[idx])
        e = int(input_data[idx+1])
        operations.append([s, e])
        idx += 2
        
    sol = Solution()

def max_collected_resources(resources)
  n = resources.length
  memo = {}
  
  def dp(r1, c1, r2, c2, r3, c3)
    return 0 if r1 == n-1 && c1 == n-1 && r2 == n-1 && c2 == n-1 && r3 == n-1 && c3 == n-1
    
    key = [r1, c1, r2, c2, r3, c3]
    return memo[key] if memo.key?(key)
    
    current_resources = 0
    visited = {}
    
    [[r1, c1], [r2, c2], [r3, c3]].each do |r, c|
      if !visited[[r, c]]
        current_resources += resources[r][c]
        visited[[r, c]] = true
      end
    end
    
    max_future = 0
    
    moves1 = [[1, 1], [1, 0], [0, 1]]
    moves2 = [[1, -1], [1, 0], [1, 1]]
    moves3 = [[-1, 1], [0, 1], [1, 1]]
    
    moves1.each do |dr1, dc1|
      nr1, nc1 = r1 + dr1, c1 + dc1
      next if nr1 >= n || nc1 >= n
      
      moves2.each do |dr2, dc2|
        nr2, nc2 = r2 + dr2, c2 + dc2
        next if nr2 >= n || nc2 < 0 || nc2 >= n
        
        moves3.each do |dr3, dc3|
          nr3, nc3 = r3 + dr3, c3 + dc3
          next if nr3 < 0 || nr3 >= n || nc3 >= n
          
          future = dp(nr1, nc1, nr2, nc2, nr3, nc3)
          max_future = [max_future, future].max
        end
      end
    end
    
    memo[key] = current_resources + max_future
    memo[key]
  end
  
  dp(0, 0, 0, n-1, n-1, 0)
end

def main
  n = gets.to_i
  resources = []
  n.times do
    row = gets.split.map(&:to_i)
    resources << row
  end
  
  result = max_collected_resources(resources)
  puts result
end

class Solution
    def computeMinimumTime(difficulty, m)
        n = difficulty.length
        min_time = Float::INFINITY
        
        difficulty.permutation do |perm|
            time = 0
            energy = 0
            y = 1
            
            perm.each do |diff|
                while energy < diff
                    time += 1
                    energy += y
                end
                energy = 0
                y += m
            end
            
            min_time = [min_time, time].min
        end
        
        min_time
    end
end

def compute_minimum_time(difficulty, m)
  n = difficulty.length
  min_total_time = Float::INFINITY
  
  difficulty.permutation.each do |p|
    current_time = 0
    y = 1
    p.each do |d|
      time_needed = (d.to_f / y).ceil
      current_time += time_needed
      y += m
    end
    min_total_time = [min_total_time, current_time].min
  end
  
  min_total_time
end

def circular_navigation(nums)
  n = nums.length
  destinations = Array.new(n)
  
  nums.each_with_index do |val, i|
    if val == 0
      destinations[i] = 0
    else
      destinations[i] = (i + val) % n
    end
  end
  
  destinations
end

from typing import List

class Analyzer:
    def maxSegmentTotal(self, measurements: List[int], k: int) -> int:
        n = len(measurements)
        max_total = float('-inf')
        
        for length in range(k, n + 1, k):
            current_sum = sum(measurements[:length])
            max_total = max(max_total, current_sum)
            
            for i in range(length, n):
                current_sum = current_sum - measurements[i - length] + measurements[i]
                max_total = max(max_total, current_sum)
        
        return max_total

            if length % k == 0

class Solution
  def minimumTotalEffort(efforts, k, op1, op2)
    n = efforts.length
    best = Float::INFINITY
    
    (0...(1 << n)).each do |mask1|
      next if mask1.to_s(2).count('1') > op1
      
      (0...(1 << n)).each do |mask2|
        next if mask2.to_s(2).count('1') > op2
        
        total = 0
        valid = true
        
        n.times do |i|
          val = efforts[i]
          
          if (mask1 & (1 << i)) != 0
            val = (val + 1) / 2
          end
          
          if (mask2 & (1 << i)) != 0
            if val >= k
              val -= k
            else
              valid = false
              break
            end
          end
          
          total += val
        end
        
        if valid
          best = [best, total].min
        end
      end
    end
    
    best
  end
end

require 'set'

def is_prime(n)
  return false if n < 2
  return true if n == 2
  return false if n % 2 == 0
  (3..Math.sqrt(n)).step(2) do |i|
    return false if n % i == 0
  end
  true
end

def min_operations(x, y)
  return -1 if is_prime(x) || is_prime(y)
  return x if x == y
  
  visited = Set.new
  queue = [[x, x]]
  
  while !queue.empty?
    current, cost = queue.shift
    
    next if visited.include?(current)
    visited.add(current)
    
    return cost if current == y
    
    [current - 1, current + 1].each do |next_val|
      next if next_val < 0 || next_val > 9
      next if visited.include?(next_val)
      next if is_prime(next_val)
      
      queue << [next_val, cost + next_val]
    end
    
    queue.sort_by! { |_, c| c }
  end
  
  -1
end

def main
  x = gets.chomp.to_i
  y = gets.chomp.to_i
  
  result = min_operations(x, y)
  puts result
end
