require 'set'
require 'heap'

class Solution
  def minOperations(x, y)
    return -1 if is_prime(x) || is_prime(y)
    
    digits_count = x.to_s.length
    
    pq = []
    def push(pq, cost, val)
      pq << [cost, val]
      idx = pq.length - 1
      while idx > 0
        parent = (idx - 1) / 2
        break if pq[parent][0] <= pq[idx][0]
        pq[parent], pq[idx] = pq[idx], pq[parent]
        idx = parent
      end
    end

    def pop(pq)
      return nil if pq.empty?
      res = pq[0]
      last = pq.pop
      if !pq.empty?
        pq[0] = last
        idx = 0
        while true
          left = 2 * idx + 1
          right = 2 * idx + 2
          smallest = idx
          smallest = left if left < pq.length && pq[left][0] < pq[smallest][0]
          smallest = right if right < pq.length && pq[right][0] < pq[smallest][0]
          break if smallest == idx
          pq[idx], pq[smallest] = pq[smallest], pq[idx]
          idx = smallest
        end
      end
      res
    end

    dist = {}
    dist[x] = x
    push(pq, x, x)

    until pq.empty?
      d, curr = pop(pq)
      next if d > (dist[curr] || Float::INFINITY)
      return d if curr == y

      s_curr = curr.to_s.rjust(digits_count, '0')
      (0...digits_count).each do |i|
        digit = s_curr[i].to_i
        
        [1, -1].each do |delta|
          new_digit = digit + delta
          if new_digit >= 0 && new_digit <= 9
            new_s = s_curr.dup
            new_s[i] = new_digit.to_s
            new_val = new_s.to_i
            
            if !is_prime(new_val)
              new_cost = d + new_val
              if !dist.key?(new_val) || new_cost < dist[new_val]
                dist[new_val] = new_cost
                push(pq, new_cost, new_val)
              end
            end
          end
        end
      end
    end

    -1
  end

  private

  def is_prime(n)
    return false if n < 2
    return true if n == 2 || n == 3
    return false if n % 2 == 0 || n % 3 == 0
    i = 5
    while i * i <= n
      return false if n % i == 0 || n % (i + 2) == 0
      i += 6
    end
    true
  end
end

def main
  input = ARGF.read
  return if input.nil? || input.strip.empty?

  if lines.length >= 2
    x = lines[0].to_i
    y = lines[1].to_i
    puts Solution.new.minOperations(x, y)
  end

class Solution
  def minimumActions(items)
    actions = 0
    
    while true

      if items.uniq.length == items.length
        break
      end
      

      if items.length >= 3
        items = items[3..-1]
      else
        items = []
      end
      
      actions += 1
    end
    
    actions
  end
end

def main
  input = gets.chomp
  items = input.split(',').map(&:to_i)
  
  solution = Solution.new
  result = solution.minimumActions(items)
  puts result
end

require 'set'

def minimum_actions(items)
  n = items.length
  actions = 0
  
  while true
    current_slice = items[(actions * 3)..-1] || []
    if current_slice.empty?
      return actions
    end
    
    if current_slice.uniq.length == current_slice.length
      return actions
    end
    
    actions += 1
    if actions * 3 >= n
      return actions
    end
  end
end

def main
  input_data = ARGF.read
  if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
    return
  end

  items = input_data.scan(/\d+/).map(&:to_i)
  
  if items.empty?
    puts 0
  else
    puts minimum_actions(items)
  end
end

def check_valid_dividers(m, plots)

  y_coords = []
  plots.each do |plot|
    y_coords << plot[1]
    y_coords << plot[3]
  end
  y_coords = y_coords.uniq.sort
  
  (0...y_coords.length).each do |i|
    ((i+1)...y_coords.length).each do |j|
      y1, y2 = y_coords[i], y_coords[j]
      next if y1 >= y2
      
      section1 = plots.select { |plot| plot[3] <= y1 }
      section2 = plots.select { |plot| plot[1] >= y1 && plot[3] <= y2 }
      section3 = plots.select { |plot| plot[1] >= y2 }
      
      if section1.length > 0 && section2.length > 0 && section3.length > 0 &&
         section1.length + section2.length + section3.length == plots.length
        return true
      end
    end
  end
  

  x_coords = []
  plots.each do |plot|
    x_coords << plot[0]
    x_coords << plot[2]
  end
  x_coords = x_coords.uniq.sort
  
  (0...x_coords.length).each do |i|
    ((i+1)...x_coords.length).each do |j|
      x1, x2 = x_coords[i], x_coords[j]
      next if x1 >= x2
      
      section1 = plots.select { |plot| plot[2] <= x1 }
      section2 = plots.select { |plot| plot[0] >= x1 && plot[2] <= x2 }
      section3 = plots.select { |plot| plot[0] >= x2 }
      
      if section1.length > 0 && section2.length > 0 && section3.length > 0 &&
         section1.length + section2.length + section3.length == plots.length
        return true
      end
    end
  end
  
  false
end

def main
  m = gets.to_i
  plots = []
  while line = gets
    line = line.strip
    break if line.empty?
    coords = line.split.map(&:to_i)
    plots << coords
  end
  
  result = check_valid_dividers(m, plots)
  puts result
end

class Solution:
    def checkValidDividers(self, m: int, plots: list[list[int]]) -> bool:
        def check(intervals):
            intervals.sort()
            n = len(intervals)
            max_end = -1
            dividers = []
            
            i = 0
            while i < n:
                current_max_end = intervals[i][1]
                j = i
                while j < n and intervals[j][0] < current_max_end:
                    current_max_end = max(current_max_end, intervals[j][1])
                    j += 1
                
                if j < n:
                    dividers.append(current_max_end)
                i = j
            
            return len(dividers) >= 2

        h_intervals = [(p[1], p[3]) for p in plots]
        v_intervals = [(p[0], p[2]) for p in plots]
        
        return check(h_intervals) or check(v_intervals)

require 'json'

class Solution
  def checkValidDividers(m, plots)
    check_horizontal(plots) || check_vertical(plots)
  end

  private

  def check_horizontal(plots)
    y_intervals = plots.map { |p| [p[1], p[3]] }
    y_intervals.sort!
    
    merged = []
    return false if y_intervals.empty?
    
    curr_start, curr_end = y_intervals[0]
    y_intervals[1..-1].each do |s, e|
      if s < curr_end
        curr_end = e if e > curr_end
      else
        merged << [curr_start, curr_end]
        curr_start, curr_end = s, e
      end
    end
    merged << [curr_start, curr_end]
    
    merged.length >= 3
  end

  def check_vertical(plots)
    x_intervals = plots.map { |p| [p[0], p[2]] }
    x_intervals.sort!
    
    merged = []
    return false if x_intervals.empty?
    
    curr_start, curr_end = x_intervals[0]
    x_intervals[1..-1].each do |s, e|
      if s < curr_end
        curr_end = e if e > curr_end
      else
        merged << [curr_start, curr_end]
        curr_start, curr_end = s, e
      end
    end
    merged << [curr_start, curr_end]
    
    merged.length >= 3
  end
end

def main
  input_data = STDIN.read
  return if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
  
  begin
    parsed = JSON.parse("[" + input_data.gsub("\n", ",") + "]") rescue nil
    if parsed.nil?
      lines = input_data.split("\n")
      m = lines[0].to_i
      plots = JSON.parse(lines[1])
    else
      m = parsed[0]
      plots = parsed[1]
    end
    
    sol = Solution.new
    result = sol.checkValidDividers(m, plots)
    puts result.to_s
  rescue
    exit(0)
  end
end

def minimize_difference(elevations)
  n = elevations.length
  

  left, right = 0, 2 * 10**9
  
  while left < right
    mid = (left + right) / 2
    
    if can_achieve_max_diff(elevations, mid)
      right = mid
    else
      left = mid + 1
    end
  end
  
  left
end

def can_achieve_max_diff(elevations, max_diff)
  n = elevations.length
  


  
  existing_values = elevations.select { |x| x != -1 }
  
  if existing_values.empty?
    return true
  end
  
  min_existing = existing_values.min
  max_existing = existing_values.max
  

  range_start = [1, min_existing - max_diff].max
  range_end = max_existing + max_diff
  

  (range_start..range_end).each do |x|
    (x..range_end).each do |y|
      if valid_assignment(elevations, x, y, max_diff)
        return true
      end
    end
  end
  
  false
end

def valid_assignment(elevations, x, y, max_diff)
  n = elevations.length
  

  missing_positions = []
  (0...n).each do |i|
    if elevations[i] == -1
      missing_positions << i
    end
  end
  

  (0...(1 << missing_positions.length)).each do |mask|
    arr = elevations.dup
    
    missing_positions.each_with_index do |pos, idx|
      if (mask >> idx) & 1 == 1
        arr[pos] = x
      else
        arr[pos] = y
      end
    end
    

    valid = true
    (0...n-1).each do |i|
      if (arr[i] - arr[i+1]).abs > max_diff
        valid = false
        break
      end
    end
    
    return true if valid
  end
  
  false
end

def main
  input = gets.strip
  elevations = input.split(',').map { |x| x.strip.to_i }
  
  result = minimize_difference(elevations)
  puts result
end

def count_ways_with_xor_treasure(maze, target)
  m = maze.length
  n = maze[0].length
  mod = 10**9 + 7
  

  dp = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { Hash.new(0) } }
  

  dp[0][0][maze[0][0]] = 1
  

  (0...m).each do |i|
    (0...n).each do |j|
      dp[i][j].each do |current_xor, count|

        if j + 1 < n
          new_xor = current_xor ^ maze[i][j + 1]
          dp[i][j + 1][new_xor] = (dp[i][j + 1][new_xor] + count) % mod
        end
        

        if i + 1 < m
          new_xor = current_xor ^ maze[i + 1][j]
          dp[i + 1][j][new_xor] = (dp[i + 1][j][new_xor] + count) % mod
        end
      end
    end
  end
  
  dp[m - 1][n - 1][target]
end

    row = line.split.map(&:to_i)
    maze << row
  end
  

  target = gets.to_i
  
  result = count_ways_with_xor_treasure(maze, target)
  puts result
end

import json
import sys

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read()
    if not input_data:
        return
    
    try:
        data = json.loads(input_data)
        maze = data["maze"]
        target = data["target"]
    except:
        return

    m = maze.length
    n = maze[0].length
    mod = 10**9 + 7
    
    dp = Array.new(n) { Hash.new(0) }
    
    dp[0][maze[0][0]] = 1
    
    (0...m).each do |i|
        new_dp = Array.new(n) { Hash.new(0) }
        (0...n).each do |j|
            current_val = maze[i][j]
            
            if i == 0 && j == 0
                new_dp[j] = dp[j]
                next
            end
            
            from_top = i > 0 ? dp[j] : {}
            from_left = j > 0 ? new_dp[j-1] : {}
            
            from_top.each do |xor_sum, count|
                new_xor = xor_sum ^ current_val
                new_dp[j][new_xor] = (new_dp[j][new_xor] + count) % mod
            end
            
            from_left.each do |xor_sum, count|
                new_xor = xor_sum ^ current_val
                new_dp[j][new_xor] = (new_dp[j][new_xor] + count) % mod
            end
        end
        dp = new_dp
    end
    
    puts dp[n-1][target] || 0
end

def main
    input_text = STDIN.read
    return if input_text.empty?

    lines = input_text.split("\n")
    maze_str = ""
    target_str = ""
    
    if input_text.include?("maze")
        maze_match = input_text.match(/maze\s*=\s*(\[\[.*?\]\])/m)
        target_match = input_text.match(/target\s*=\s*(\d+)/)
        if maze_match && target_match
            maze = JSON.parse(maze_match[1])
            target = target_match[1].to_i
            puts countWaysWithXorTreasure(maze, target)
        end
    else
        begin
            data = JSON.parse(input_text)
            puts countWaysWithXorTreasure(data["maze"], data["target"])
        rescue
        end
    end
end

def countWaysWithXorTreasure(maze, target)
    m = maze.length
    n = maze[0].length
    mod = 1_000_000_007
    
    dp = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { Hash.new(0) } }
    
    dp[0][0][maze[0][0]] = 1
    
    (0...m).each do |r|
        (0...n).each do |c|
            dp[r][c].each do |current_xor, count|
                if r + 1 < m
                    next_xor = current_xor ^ maze[r+1][c]
                    dp[r+1][c][next_xor] = (dp[r+1][c][next_xor] + count) % mod
                end
                if c + 1 < n
                    next_xor = current_xor ^ maze[r][c+1]
                    dp[r][c+1][next_xor] = (dp[r][c+1][next_xor] + count) % mod
                end
            end
        end
    end
    
    dp[m-1][n-1][target] || 0
end

import json
import sys

class Solution
  def maximizeTotalBandwidth(connections, k)
    n = connections.length + 1
    adj = Array.new(n) { [] }
    connections.each do |u, v, b|
      adj[u] << [v, b]
      adj[v] << [u, b]
    end

    memo = {}

    dfs = lambda do |u, p|
      gains = []
      total_base = 0
      
      adj[u].each do |v, b|
        next if v == p
        f0, f1 = dfs.call(v, u)
        total_base += f0
        diff = b + f1 - f0
        gains << diff if diff > 0
      end
      
      gains.sort! { |a, b| b <=> a }
      
      sum_gains_k = 0
      sum_gains_k_minus_1 = 0
      
      gains.each_with_index do |val, idx|
        if idx < k - 1
          sum_gains_k += val
          sum_gains_k_minus_1 += val
        elsif idx < k
          sum_gains_k += val
        else
          break
        end
      end
      
      return [total_base + sum_gains_k, total_base + sum_gains_k_minus_1]
    end

    result = dfs.call(0, -1)
    result[0]
  end
end

def main
  input_data = STDIN.read
  if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
    return
  end
  
  begin
    data = JSON.parse(input_data)
    connections = data["connections"]
    k = data["k"]
    
    sol = Solution.new
    puts sol.maximizeTotalBandwidth(connections, k)
  rescue
    begin
      lines = input_data.split("\n")
      return if lines.empty?
      connections = JSON.parse(lines[0])
      k = lines[1].to_i
      sol = Solution.new
      puts sol.maximizeTotalBandwidth(connections, k)
    rescue
    end
  end
end

def gcd(a, b)
  while b != 0
    a, b = b, a % b
  end
  a
end

def lcm(a, b)
  (a * b) / gcd(a, b)
end

def find(parent, x)
  if parent[x] != x
    parent[x] = find(parent, parent[x])
  end
  parent[x]
end

def union(parent, rank, x, y)
  px = find(parent, x)
  py = find(parent, y)
  
  if px == py
    return
  end
  
  if rank[px] < rank[py]
    parent[px] = py
  elsif rank[px] > rank[py]
    parent[py] = px
  else
    parent[py] = px
    rank[px] += 1
  end
end

def count_groups(elements, limit)
  n = elements.length
  parent = (0...n).to_a
  rank = Array.new(n, 0)
  
  for i in 0...n
    for j in (i+1)...n
      if lcm(elements[i], elements[j]) <= limit
        union(parent, rank, i, j)
      end
    end
  end
  
  groups = Set.new
  for i in 0...n
    groups.add(find(parent, i))
  end
  
  groups.size
end

def main
  input = STDIN.read.strip.split("\n")
  elements = input[0].split.map(&:to_i)
  limit = input[1].to_i
  
  result = count_groups(elements, limit)
  puts result
end

require 'json'

class DSU
  attr_reader :count
  def initialize(n)
    @parent = Array.new(n) { |i| i }
    @count = n
  end

  def find(i)
    return @parent[i] if @parent[i] == i
    @parent[i] = find(@parent[i])
  end

  def union(i, j)
    root_i = find(i)
    root_j = find(j)
    if root_i != root_j
      @parent[root_i] = root_j
      @count -= 1
    end
  end
end

class Solution
  def countGroups(elements, limit)
    n = elements.length
    valid_elements = []
    large_elements_count = 0

    elements.each do |x|
      if x <= limit
        valid_elements << x
      else
        large_elements_count += 1
      end
    end

    return large_elements_count if valid_elements.empty?

    m = valid_elements.length
    dsu = DSU.new(m)
    
    val_to_idx = {}
    valid_elements.each_with_index { |x, i| val_to_idx[x] = i }

    min_val_for_gcd = Array.new(limit + 1, Float::INFINITY)

    valid_elements.each_with_index do |x, i|
      (1..Math.sqrt(x).to_i).each do |g|
        if x % g == 0
          min_val_for_gcd[g] = x if x < min_val_for_gcd[g]
          g2 = x / g
          min_val_for_gcd[g2] = x if x < min_val_for_gcd[g2]
        end
      end
    end

    valid_elements.each_with_index do |x, i|
      (1..Math.sqrt(x).to_i).each do |g|
        if x % g == 0
          [g, x / g].each do |gcd_val|
            other_min = min_val_for_gcd[gcd_val]
            if other_min != Float::INFINITY
              if (x.to_f * other_min / gcd_val) <= limit
                dsu.union(i, val_to_idx[other_min])
              end
            end
          end
        end
      end
    end

    dsu.count + large_elements_count
  end
end

def main
  input_data = STDIN.read
  return if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
  
  begin

    elements_part = parts[0].split('elements =')[1].strip
    elements = JSON.parse(elements_part.gsub(/,$/, ''))
    limit = parts[1].strip.to_i
    
    sol = Solution.new
    puts sol.countGroups(elements, limit)
  rescue
  end
end

def largest_parallelogram_area(locations)
    n = locations.length
    return -1 if n < 4
    
    max_area = -1
    

    (0...n).to_a.combination(4) do |indices|
        points = indices.map { |i| locations[i] }
        

        xs = points.map { |p| p[0] }.uniq.sort
        ys = points.map { |p| p[1] }.uniq.sort
        
        next if xs.length != 2 || ys.length != 2
        

        expected_points = []
        xs.each do |x|
            ys.each do |y|
                expected_points << [x, y]
            end
        end
        
        next if points.sort != expected_points.sort
        

        min_x, max_x = xs[0], xs[1]
        min_y, max_y = ys[0], ys[1]
        

        valid = true
        locations.each_with_index do |point, i|
            next if indices.include?(i)
            
            x, y = point[0], point[1]
            if x >= min_x && x <= max_x && y >= min_y && y <= max_y
                valid = false
                break
            end
        end
        
        if valid
            area = (max_x - min_x) * (max_y - min_y)
            max_area = [max_area, area].max
        end
    end
    
    max_area
end

def main
    input = STDIN.read.strip
    locations = eval(input)
    result = largest_parallelogram_area(locations)
    puts result
end

  def largestParallelogramArea(locations)
    n = locations.length
    max_area = -1
    
    (0...n).to_a.combination(4) do |indices|
      pts = indices.map { |i| locations[i] }
      
      xs = pts.map { |p| p[0] }.uniq.sort
      ys = pts.map { |p| p[1] }.uniq.sort

      x1, x2 = xs
      y1, y2 = ys
      
      rect_pts = [[x1, y1], [x1, y2], [x2, y1], [x2, y2]]

      locations.each_with_index do |loc, idx|

          valid = false
          break
        end
      end
      
      if valid
        area = (x2 - x1) * (y2 - y1)
        max_area = [max_area, area].max
      end
    end
    
    max_area
  end
end

def count_good_mosaic_sequences(n, m, k)
    MOD = 10**9 + 7
    
    return 0 if k > n - 1
    
    dp = Array.new(n + 1) { Array.new(k + 1, 0) }
    dp[1][0] = m
    
    (2..n).each do |i|
        (0..k).each do |j|
            dp[i][j] = (dp[i-1][j] * (m - 1)) % MOD
            if j > 0
                dp[i][j] = (dp[i][j] + dp[i-1][j-1]) % MOD
            end
        end
    end
    
    dp[n][k]
end

    result = count_good_mosaic_sequences(n, m, k)

MOD = 10**9 + 7

class MosaicSolution
  def countGoodMosaicSequences(n, m, k)
    return 0 if k >= n
    return m if n == 1 && k == 0
    return 0 if n == 1 && k > 0

    fact = Array.new(n + 1)
    inv = Array.new(n + 1)
    fact[0] = 1
    (1..n).each { |i| fact[i] = (fact[i - 1] * i) % MOD }
    inv[n] = power(fact[n], MOD - 2)
    (n - 1).downto(0) { |i| inv[i] = (inv[i + 1] * (i + 1)) % MOD }

    def nCr(n, r, fact, inv)
      return 0 if r < 0 || r > n
      (fact[n] * inv[r] % MOD * inv[n - r] % MOD) % MOD
    end

    ways_to_choose_positions = nCr(n - 1, k, fact, inv)
    ways_to_color = (m * power(m - 1, n - 1 - k)) % MOD

    (ways_to_choose_positions * ways_to_color) % MOD
  end

  private

  def power(a, b)
    res = 1
    a %= MOD
    while b > 0
      res = (res * a) % MOD if b % 2 == 1
      a = (a * a) % MOD
      b /= 2
    end
    res
  end
end

def main
  input = STDIN.read.split.map(&:to_i)
  return if input.empty?
  n, m, k = input[0], input[1], input[2]
  sol = MosaicSolution.new
  puts sol.countGoodMosaicSequences(n, m, k)
end

class Solution
  def computeLargestString(sequence, numAgents)
    n = sequence.length
    max_string = ""
    
    def generate_partitions(sequence, numAgents, start, current_partition, all_partitions)
      if current_partition.length == numAgents
        if start == sequence.length
          all_partitions << current_partition.dup
        end
        return
      end
      
      if current_partition.length == numAgents - 1
        if start < sequence.length
          current_partition << sequence[start..-1]
          all_partitions << current_partition.dup
          current_partition.pop
        end
        return
      end
      
      (start...sequence.length).each do |i|
        current_partition << sequence[start..i]
        generate_partitions(sequence, numAgents, i + 1, current_partition, all_partitions)
        current_partition.pop
      end
    end
    
    all_partitions = []
    generate_partitions(sequence, numAgents, 0, [], all_partitions)
    
    all_partitions.each do |partition|
      partition.each do |substring|
        if substring > max_string
          max_string = substring
        end
      end
    end
    
    max_string
  end
end

end

class Solution
  def computeLargestString(sequence, num_agents)
    n = sequence.length
    return sequence if num_agents == 1
    
    max_len = n - num_agents + 1
    best = ""
    
    (0...n).each do |i|
      len = [max_len, n - i].min
      current = sequence[i, len]
      best = current if current > best
    end
    
    best
  end
end

class Solution
  def hasPattern(dna, pattern)
    star_index = pattern.index('*')
    prefix = pattern[0...star_index]
    suffix = pattern[(star_index + 1)..-1]
    
    (0..dna.length).each do |i|
      next if i + prefix.length > dna.length
      next unless dna[i, prefix.length] == prefix
      
      start_pos = i + prefix.length
      (start_pos..dna.length).each do |j|
        next if j + suffix.length > dna.length
        if dna[j, suffix.length] == suffix
          return true
        end
      end
    end
    
    false
  end
end

import json

class Solution
  def hasPattern(dna, pattern)
    parts = pattern.split('*', -1)
    prefix = parts[0]
    suffix = parts[1]
    
    n = dna.length
    (0...n).each do |i|
      (i...n).each do |j|
        sub = dna[i..j]
        if sub.start_with?(prefix) && sub.end_with?(suffix)
          if sub.length >= (prefix.length + suffix.length)
            return true
          end
        end
      end
    end
    false
  end
end

        n = len(elements)
        count = 0
        
        for i in range(1, n-1):
            for j in range(i+1, n):
                elements1 = elements[:i]
                elements2 = elements[i:j]
                elements3 = elements[j:]
                
                is_prefix1 = len(elements1) <= len(elements2) and elements2[:len(elements1)] == elements1
                is_prefix2 = len(elements2) <= len(elements3) and elements3[:len(elements2)] == elements2
                
                if is_prefix1 or is_prefix2:
                    count += 1
        
        return count

def elegant_divisions(elements)
    n = elements.length
    return 0 if n < 3
    
    count = 0
    
    (1...n-1).each do |i|
        (i+1...n).each do |j|
            elements1 = elements[0...i]
            elements2 = elements[i...j]
            elements3 = elements[j...n]
            
            if is_prefix(elements1, elements2) || is_prefix(elements2, elements3)
                count += 1
            end
        end
    end
    
    count
end

def is_prefix(prefix, array)
    return false if prefix.length > array.length
    (0...prefix.length).each do |i|
        return false if prefix[i] != array[i]
    end
    true
end

  def elegantDivisions(elements)
    n = elements.length
    return 0 if n < 3
    
    count = 0
    (1..n-2).each do |i|
      (i+1..n-1).each do |j|
        e1 = elements[0...i]
        e2 = elements[i...j]
        e3 = elements[j...n]
        
        is_elegant = false
        
        if e1.length <= e2.length
          match1 = true
          e1.each_with_index do |val, idx|
            if val != e2[idx]
              match1 = false
              break
            end
          end
          is_elegant = true if match1
        end
        
        if !is_elegant && e2.length <= e3.length
          match2 = true
          e2.each_with_index do |val, idx|
            if val != e3[idx]
              match2 = false
              break
            end
          end
          is_elegant = true if match2
        end
        
        count += 1 if is_elegant
      end
    end
    count
  end
end

def kadane(arr)
  return 0 if arr.empty?
  
  max_sum = arr[0]
  current_sum = arr[0]
  
  (1...arr.length).each do |i|
    current_sum = [arr[i], current_sum + arr[i]].max
    max_sum = [max_sum, current_sum].max
  end
  
  max_sum
end

def find_max_consecutive_sum(sales)
  return 0 if sales.empty?
  

  max_sum = kadane(sales)
  

  unique_values = sales.uniq
  
  unique_values.each do |val|

    filtered = sales.reject { |x| x == val }
    

    next if filtered.empty?
    

    current_max = kadane(filtered)
    max_sum = [max_sum, current_max].max
  end
  
  max_sum
end

            max_so_far = -float('inf')

                if sales[i] == y:

                val = sales[i]
                temp_sum = max(val, temp_sum + val)
                current_max = max(current_max, temp_sum)
            
            ans = max(ans, current_max)
            
        return ans

                if j < m - 1:

        return overall_max

class Solution
  def min_consecutive_length(elements, max_changes)
    n = elements.length
    
    def can_achieve(s, target, max_changes)
      n = s.length
      

      changes_a = count_changes_needed(s, 'A', target)
      

      changes_b = count_changes_needed(s, 'B', target)
      
      [changes_a, changes_b].min <= max_changes
    end
    
    def count_changes_needed(s, char, target)
      changes = 0
      i = 0
      n = s.length
      
      while i < n
        if s[i] == char

          run_start = i
          while i < n && s[i] == char
            i += 1
          end
          run_length = i - run_start
          

          if run_length > target


            changes += (run_length - 1) / (target + 1)
          end
        else
          i += 1
        end
      end
      
      changes
    end
    

    left, right = 1, n
    
    while left < right
      mid = (left + right) / 2
      if can_achieve(elements, mid, max_changes)
        right = mid
      else
        left = mid + 1
      end
    end
    
    left
  end
end

import json
import sys

class Solution
  def min_consecutive_length(elements, max_changes)
    n = elements.length
    
    low = 1
    high = n
    ans = n
    
    while low <= high
      mid = (low + high) / 2
      if mid == 1
        cost = check_one(elements)
        if cost <= max_changes
          ans = mid
          high = mid - 1
        else
          low = mid + 1
        end
      else
        if check_k(elements, mid) <= max_changes
          ans = mid
          high = mid - 1
        else
          low = mid + 1
        end
      end
    end
    ans
  end

  def check_one(s)
    cost_a = 0
    cost_b = 0
    s.each_char.with_index do |char, i|
      expected_a = (i % 2 == 0 ? 'A' : 'B')
      expected_b = (i % 2 == 0 ? 'B' : 'A')
      cost_a += 1 if char != expected_a
      cost_b += 1 if char != expected_b
    end
    [cost_a, cost_b].min
  end

  def check_k(s, k)
    total_changes = 0
    i = 0
    n = s.length
    while i < n
      j = i
      while j < n && s[j] == s[i]
        j += 1
      end
      len = j - i
      total_changes += len / (k + 1)
      i = j
    end
    total_changes
  end
end

def main
  input_data = STDIN.read
  return if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
  
  lines = input_data.split("\n")
  lines.each do |line|
    next if line.strip.empty?
    begin
      data = JSON.parse(line)
      s = data["s"]
      max_changes = data["maxChanges"]
      sol = Solution.new
      puts sol.min_consecutive_length(s, max_changes)
    rescue
      next
    end
  end
end

    (0...n-6).each do |a|
        (a+2...n-4).each do |b|
            (b+2...n-2).each do |c|
                (c+2...n).each do |d|
                    if plants[a] * plants[c] == plants[b] * plants[d]
                        count += 1
                    end
                end
            end
        end
    end
    
    count
end

    result = count_balanced_quadruplets(plants)

import sys
from collections import Counter

class Solution:
    def countSubsequencesWithCentralMode(self, elements: list[int]) -> int:
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        n = len(elements)
        
        def nCr(n, r):
            if r < 0 or r > n:
                return 0
            if r == 0 or r == n:
                return 1
            if r > n
                r = n - r
            
            res = 1
            for i in range(r):
                res = res * (n - i)
            return res

        total_freq = Counter(elements)
        left_freq = Counter()
        right_freq = total_freq.copy()
        
        ans = 0
        total_subsequences_5 = nCr(n, 5)
        
        invalid = 0
        
        for i in range(n):
            x = elements[i]
            right_freq[x] -= 1
            
            l_cnt = left_freq[x]
            r_cnt = right_freq[x]
            n_l = i
            n_r = n - 1 - i
            
            total_ways = nCr(n_l, 2) * nCr(n_r, 2)
            
            case3 = nCr(l_cnt, 2) * nCr(r_cnt, 2)
            case2_l = nCr(l_cnt, 2) * nCr(n_r - r_cnt, 1) * r_cnt + nCr(l_cnt, 2) * nCr(r_cnt, 2)
            case2_r = nCr(r_cnt, 2) * nCr(n_l - l_cnt, 1) * l_cnt + nCr(l_cnt, 2) * nCr(r_cnt, 2)
            
            good = 0
            good += nCr(l_cnt, 2) * nCr(r_cnt, 2)
            good += nCr(l_cnt, 2) * (nCr(n_r, 2) - nCr(r_cnt, 2))
            good += nCr(r_cnt, 2) * (nCr(n_l, 2) - nCr(l_cnt, 2))
            good += (l_cnt * r_cnt) * ( (n_l - l_cnt) * (n_r - r_cnt) )
            
            others_l = 0
            for val, freq in left_freq.items():
                if val == x: continue
                others_l += nCr(freq, 2)
            
            others_r = 0
            for val, freq in right_freq.items():
                if val == x: continue
                others_r += nCr(freq, 2)
            
            good += l_cnt * (n_r - r_cnt) * (n_l - l_cnt) - l_cnt * others_r
            good += r_cnt * (n_l - l_cnt) * (n_r - r_cnt) - r_cnt * others_l
            
            ans = (ans + good) % MOD
            left_freq[x] += 1
            
        return ans

def main():
    import json
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    if not input_data:
        return
    try:
        elements = json.loads(input_data)
    except:
        return
    sol = Solution()
    print(sol.countSubsequencesWithCentralMode(elements))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

MOD = 1_000_000_000 + 7

def nCr(n, r)
  return 0 if r < 0 || r > n
  return 1 if r == 0 || r == n
  if r > n / 2
    r = n - r
  end
  num = 1
  den = 1
  (0...r).each do |i|
    num = (num * (n - i))
    den = (den * (i + 1))
  end
  num / den
end

def solve
  input = STDIN.read.split
  return if input.empty?
  
  elements = input.map(&:to_i)
  n = elements.length
  
  counts = Hash.new(0)
  elements.each { |x| counts[x] += 1 }
  
  left_counts = Hash.new(0)
  right_counts = counts.clone
  
  total_ans = 0
  
  (0...n).each do |i|
    x = elements[i]
    right_counts[x] -= 1
    
    ln = i
    rn = n - 1 - i
    
    lc = left_counts[x]
    rc = right_counts[x]
    
    ways = nCr(ln, 2) * nCr(rn, 2)
    
    bad = 0
    
    counts.each do |v, total_v|
      next if v == x
      lv = left_counts[v]
      rv = right_counts[v]
      
      bad += nCr(lv, 2) * nCr(rv, 2)
      
      bad += nCr(lv, 2) * rv * (rn - rv)
      bad += nCr(rv, 2) * lv * (ln - lv)
      
      bad += nCr(lv, 2) * nCr(rv, 1) * (rc)
      bad += nCr(rv, 2) * nCr(lv, 1) * (lc)
      
      bad += lv * rv * (ln - lv) * (rn - rv)
      bad -= lv * rv * (lc * (rn - rv - rc) + rc * (ln - lv - lc) + lc * rc)
    end
    
    total_ans = (total_ans + ways - bad) % MOD
    
    left_counts[x] += 1
  end
  
  puts total_ans % MOD
end

  solve
end

def gcd(a, b)
  while b != 0
    a, b = b, a % b
  end
  a
end

def lcm(a, b)
  (a * b) / gcd(a, b)
end

def array_gcd(arr)
  result = arr[0]
  (1...arr.length).each do |i|
    result = gcd(result, arr[i])
  end
  result
end

def array_lcm(arr)
  result = arr[0]
  (1...arr.length).each do |i|
    result = lcm(result, arr[i])
  end
  result
end

def product(arr)
  result = 1
  arr.each { |x| result *= x }
  result
end

def is_metric_balanced(arr)
  prod = product(arr)
  g = array_gcd(arr)
  l = array_lcm(arr)
  prod == l * g
end

def max_length(quantities)
  n = quantities.length
  max_len = 0
  
  (0...n).each do |i|
    (i...n).each do |j|
      subseq = quantities[i..j]
      if is_metric_balanced(subseq)
        max_len = [max_len, subseq.length].max
      end
    end
  end
  
  max_len
end

  input = gets.chomp
  quantities = input.split(',').map(&:to_i)
  result = max_length(quantities)
  puts result

    end
  end

  puts max_len

from typing import List

class Solution:
    def maximumEnergy(self, energy: List[List[int]], k: int) -> int:
        events = []
        for start, end, e in energy:
            events.append((start, e))
            events.append((end + 1, -e))
        
        events.sort()
        
        max_energy = 0
        current_energy = 0
        i = 0
        
        while i < len(events):
            pos = events[i][0]
            
            while i < len(events) and events[i][0] == pos:
                current_energy += events[i][1]
                i += 1
            
            if i < len(events):
                next_pos = events[i][0]
                segment_length = next_pos - pos
                
                if segment_length >= k:
                    max_energy = max(max_energy, current_energy * k)
                else:
                    max_energy = max(max_energy, current_energy * segment_length)
            else:
                max_energy = max(max_energy, current_energy * k)
        
        return max_energy

        energy.append([start, end, e])
    k = int(input())
    
    solution = Solution()
    result = solution.maximumEnergy(energy, k)
    print(result)

import sys
import json

class Solution:
    def maximumEnergy(self, energy: list[list[int]], k: int) -> int:
        energy.sort()
        n = len(energy)
        prefix_sum = [0] * (n + 1)
        for i in range(n):
            length = energy[i][1] - energy[i][0] + 1
            prefix_sum[i+1] = prefix_sum[i] + length * energy[i][2]
        
        ans = 0
        
        j = 0
        for i in range(n):
            start = energy[i][0]
            end = start + k - 1
            while j < n and energy[j][1] <= end:
                j += 1
            
            current_energy = prefix_sum[j] - prefix_sum[i]
            if j < n and energy[j][0] <= end:
                overlap = end - energy[j][0] + 1
                current_energy += overlap * energy[j][2]
            ans = max(ans, current_energy)
            
        j = n - 1
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            end = energy[i][1]
            start = end - k + 1
            while j >= 0 and energy[j][0] >= start:
                j -= 1
            
            current_energy = prefix_sum[i + 1] - prefix_sum[j + 1]
            if j >= 0 and energy[j][1] >= start:
                overlap = energy[j][1] - start + 1
                current_energy += overlap * energy[j][2]
            ans = max(ans, current_energy)
            
        return ans

    try:
        energy = json.loads(input_data[0])
        k = int(input_data[1])
        sol = Solution()
        print(sol.maximumEnergy(energy, k))
    except:
        pass

require 'json'

class Solution
  def maximumEnergy(energy, k)
    energy.sort_by! { |e| e[0] }
    n = energy.length
    prefix_sum = Array.new(n + 1, 0)
    prefix_len = Array.new(n + 1, 0)

    energy.each_with_index do |(s, e, val), i|
      len = e - s + 1
      prefix_sum[i + 1] = prefix_sum[i] + val.to_i * len
      prefix_len[i + 1] = prefix_len[i] + len
    end

    max_e = 0

    energy.each_with_index do |(s, e, val), i|
      win_start = s
      win_end = s + k - 1
      
      idx = energy.bsearch_index { |x| x[0] > win_end } || n
      idx -= 1
      
      current_energy = 0
      if idx >= i
        current_energy = prefix_sum[idx] - prefix_sum[i]
        last_seg = energy[idx]
        overlap_end = [win_end, last_seg[1]].min
        current_energy += (overlap_end - last_seg[0] + 1) * last_seg[2]
      end
      max_e = [max_e, current_energy].max
    end

    energy.each_with_index do |(s, e, val), i|
      win_end = e
      win_start = e - k + 1
      
      idx = energy.bsearch_index { |x| x[1] >= win_start } || n
      
      current_energy = 0
      if idx <= i
        current_energy = prefix_sum[i + 1] - prefix_sum[idx + 1]
        first_seg = energy[idx]
        overlap_start = [win_start, first_seg[0]].max
        current_energy += (first_seg[1] - overlap_start + 1) * first_seg[2]
      end
      max_e = [max_e, current_energy].max
    end

    max_e
  end
end

def main
  input_data = STDIN.read
  return if input_data.nil? || input_data.empty?
  
  begin
    parsed = JSON.parse("[" + input_data.gsub(']', '],').gsub(',,', ',').chomp(',') + "]")
    energy = parsed[0]
    k = parsed[1]
    
    sol = Solution.new
    puts sol.maximumEnergy(energy, k)
  rescue
    input_lines = input_data.split("\n")
    return if input_lines.empty?
    energy = JSON.parse(input_lines[0])
    k = input_lines[1].to_i
    sol = Solution.new
    puts sol.maximumEnergy(energy, k)
  end
end

def longest_height_subsequence(heights)
  n = heights.length
  memo = {}
  
  def dp(i, prev_idx, prev_diff)
    return 0 if i == n
    
    key = [i, prev_idx, prev_diff]
    return memo[key] if memo.has_key?(key)
    

    result = dp(i + 1, prev_idx, prev_diff)
    

    if prev_idx == -1

      result = [result, 1 + dp(i + 1, i, Float::INFINITY)].max
    else
      curr_diff = (heights[i] - heights[prev_idx]).abs
      if curr_diff <= prev_diff
        result = [result, 1 + dp(i + 1, i, curr_diff)].max
      end
    end
    
    memo[key] = result
    result
  end
  
  dp(0, -1, Float::INFINITY)
end

def main
  input = gets.strip
  heights = input.split(',').map(&:to_i)
  puts longest_height_subsequence(heights)
end

import itertools
import math

def distance(p1, p2):
    return math.sqrt((p1[0] - p2[0])**2 + (p1[1] - p2[1])**2)

def segment_length(seg):
    return distance((seg[0], seg[1]), (seg[2], seg[3]))

def solve(segments, S, T):
    n = len(segments)
    if n == 0:
        return 0.0
    
    min_time = float('inf')
    

    for perm in itertools.permutations(range(n)):

        for mask in range(1 << n):
            time = 0.0
            current_pos = (0, 0)
            
            for i in range(n):
                seg_idx = perm[i]
                seg = segments[seg_idx]
                

                if mask & (1 << i):
                    start_point = (seg[0], seg[1])
                    end_point = (seg[2], seg[3])
                else:
                    start_point = (seg[2], seg[3])
                    end_point = (seg[0], seg[1])
                

                move_dist = distance(current_pos, start_point)
                time += move_dist / S
                

                sketch_dist = segment_length(seg)
                time += sketch_dist / T
                

                current_pos = end_point
            
            min_time = min(min_time, time)
    
    return min_time

def main():
    line = input().split()
    N = int(line[0])
    S = int(line[1])
    T = int(line[2])
    
    segments = []
    for _ in range(N):
        line = input().split()
        A, B, C, D = int(line[0]), int(line[1]), int(line[2]), int(line[3])
        segments.append((A, B, C, D))
    
    result = solve(segments, S, T)
    print(result)

import sys
import math
import itertools

def dist(p1, p2):
    return math.sqrt((p1[0] - p2[0])**2 + (p1[1] - p2[1])**2)

def main():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data:
        return
    
    N = int(input_data[0])
    S = float(input_data[1])
    T = float(input_data[2])
    
    segments = []
    idx = 3
    for _ in range(N):
        a = int(input_data[idx])
        b = int(input_data[idx+1])
        c = int(input_data[idx+2])
        d = int(input_data[idx+3])
        segments.append(((a, b), (c, d)))
        idx += 4
        
    draw_times = []
    for seg in segments:
        draw_times.append(dist(seg[0], seg[1]) / T)
    
    total_draw_time = sum(draw_times)
    min_move_time = float('inf')
    
    indices = list(range(N))
    for p in itertools.permutations(indices):
        for i in range(1 << N):
            current_pos = (0, 0)
            current_move_time = 0.0
            
            for j in range(N):
                seg_idx = p[j]
                seg = segments[seg_idx]
                
                if (i >> j) & 1:
                    start_pt = seg[0]
                    end_pt = seg[1]
                else:
                    start_pt = seg[1]
                    end_pt = seg[0]
                
                current_move_time += dist(current_pos, start_pt) / S
                current_pos = end_pt
            
            if current_move_time < min_move_time:
                min_move_time = current_move_time
                
    print(f"{total_draw_time + min_move_time:.12f}")

        seg = segments[idx]
        if (mask & (1 << i)) == 0
          x1, y1, x2, y2 = seg[0], seg[1], seg[2], seg[3]
        else
          x1, y1, x2, y2 = seg[2], seg[3], seg[0], seg[1]
        end

        current_time += dist(cx, cy, x1, y1) / s
        current_time += dist(x1, y1, x2, y2) / t
        cx, cy = x2, y2
      end

      min_time = [min_time, current_time].min
    end
  end

  puts sprintf("%.12f", min_time)
end

def min_cost_for_capacity(c, d, e, f, target)
  return 0 if target <= 0
  
  min_cost = Float::INFINITY
  

  max_r = (target + c - 1) / c
  
  (0..max_r).each do |r_units|
    remaining = target - r_units * c
    remaining = [remaining, 0].max
    
    m_units = (remaining + e - 1) / e
    
    cost = r_units * d + m_units * f
    min_cost = [min_cost, cost].min
  end
  
  min_cost
end

def can_achieve_capacity(servers, target, budget)
  total_cost = 0
  
  servers.each do |c, d, e, f|
    cost = min_cost_for_capacity(c, d, e, f, target)
    return false if cost == Float::INFINITY
    total_cost += cost
    return false if total_cost > budget
  end
  
  total_cost <= budget
end

      right = mid - 1
    end

end

import sys

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data:
        return
    N = int(input_data[0])
    Y = int(input_data[1])
    
    servers = []
    idx = 2
    for _ in range(N):
        c = int(input_data[idx])
        d = int(input_data[idx+1])
        e = int(input_data[idx+2])
        f = int(input_data[idx+3])
        servers.append((c, d, e, f))
        idx += 4

    def get_min_cost(target, c, d, e, f):
        if target <= 0:
            return 0
        
        limit_r = (target + c - 1)
        min_cost = float('inf')
        
        step = max(1, c)
        for nr in range(min(limit_r + 1, 101)):
            rem = max(0, target - nr * c)
            ne = (rem + e - 1)
            min_cost = min(min_cost, nr * d + ne * f)
            
        limit_e = (target + e - 1)
        for ne in range(min(limit_e + 1, 101)):
            rem = max(0, target - ne * e)
            nr = (rem + c - 1)
            min_cost = min(min_cost, nr * d + ne * f)
        
        ratio_r = d / c
        ratio_m = f / e
        
        if ratio_r < ratio_m:
            nr = target
            for i in range(max(0, nr - 100), nr + 1):
                rem = max(0, target - i * c)
                ne = (rem + e - 1)
                min_cost = min(min_cost, i * d + ne * f)
        else:
            ne = target
            for i in range(max(0, ne - 100), ne + 1):
                rem = max(0, target - i * e)
                nr = (rem + c - 1)
                min_cost = min(min_cost, i * f + nr * d)
                
        return min_cost

    def check(target):
        total_cost = 0
        for c, d, e, f in servers:
            total_cost += get_min_cost(target, c, d, e, f)
            if total_cost > Y:
                return False
        return total_cost <= Y

    low = 0
    high = Y * 100 + 100
    ans = 0
    
    while low <= high:
        mid = (low + high)
        if mid == 0:
            ans = max(ans, 0)
            low = 1
            continue
        if check(mid):
            ans = mid
            low = mid + 1
        else:
            high = mid - 1
            
    print(ans)

def check(target, n, y, servers)
  total_cost = 0
  servers.each do |c, d, e, f|
    min_cost_for_server = Float::INFINITY
    
    max_r = (target + c - 1) / c
    0.upto(max_r) do |r_count|
      rem_tasks = target - (r_count * c)
      m_count = rem_tasks > 0 ? (rem_tasks + e - 1) / e : 0
      cost = r_count * d + m_count * f
      min_cost_for_server = cost if cost < min_cost_for_server
      break if m_count == 0
    end
    
    total_cost += min_cost_for_server
    return false if total_cost > y
  end
  total_cost <= y
end

def solve
  line1 = gets.split.map(&:to_i)
  n, k, x = line1[0], line1[1], line1[2]
  t = gets.split.map(&:to_i)

  inf = 10**25
  dp = Array.new(n + 1, inf)
  dp[0] = 0

  (0...n).each do |i|
    next if dp[i] == inf
    
    current_time = 0
    (1..k).each do |count|
      j = i + count
      break if j > n
      
      last_task_ready = t[j-1]
      
      min_start_time = last_task_ready
      
      cost_to_reach_j = 0
      (i...j).each do |idx|
        cost_to_reach_j += (min_start_time - t[idx])
      end
      
      current_dp_val = dp[i]
      prev_finish_time = (current_dp_val >> 60)
      prev_cost = current_dp_val & ((1 << 60) - 1)
      
      actual_start_time = [min_start_time, prev_finish_time].max
      added_delay = (actual_start_time - min_start_time) * (j - i)
      
      total_cost = prev_cost + cost_to_reach_j + added_delay
      next_finish_time = actual_start_time + x
      
      new_val = (next_finish_time << 60) | total_cost
      
      if dp[j] == inf
        dp[j] = new_val
      else
        existing_cost = dp[j] & ((1 << 60) - 1)
        if total_cost < existing_cost
          dp[j] = new_val
        elsif total_cost == existing_cost
          existing_finish = dp[j] >> 60
          if next_finish_time < existing_finish
            dp[j] = new_val
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end

  puts dp[n] & ((1 << 60) - 1)
end

  solve
end

def main
  n_str = gets
  return if n_str.nil?
  n = n_str.to_i
  
  adj = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
  in_degree = Hash.new(0)
  out_degree = Hash.new(0)
  nodes = []
  
  n.times do
    r = gets.strip
    u = r[0]
    v = r[1]
    adj[u] << v
    out_degree[u] += 1
    in_degree[v] += 1
    nodes << u << v
  end
  
  nodes.uniq!
  
  parent = {}
  nodes.each { |node| parent[node] = node }
  
  def find(parent, i)
    if parent[i] == i
      return i
    end
    parent[i] = find(parent, parent[i])
    return parent[i]
  end
  
  def union(parent, i, j)
    root_i = find(parent, i)
    root_j = find(parent, j)
    if root_i != root_j
      parent[root_i] = root_j
    end
  end
  
  adj.each do |u, neighbors|
    neighbors.each do |v|
      union(parent, u, v)
    end
  end
  
  components = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
  nodes.each do |node|
    root = find(parent, node)
    components[root] << node
  end
  
  total_strings = 0
  
  components.each do |root, members|
    net_out = 0
    has_edges = false
    
    members.each do |node|
      if out_degree[node] > in_degree[node]
        net_out += (out_degree[node] - in_degree[node])
      end
      if out_degree[node] > 0 || in_degree[node] > 0
        has_edges = true
      end
    end
    
    if !has_edges
      next
    end
    
    if net_out == 0
      total_strings += 1
    else
      total_strings += net_out
    end
  end
  
  puts total_strings
end

            char_count_after[B[k]] += 1
        end
        
        (0...j).each do |i|

def count_symmetrical_triples(B):
    n = B.length
    if n < 3:
        return 0
    
    char_counts = Hash.new(0)
    B.each_char { |c| char_counts[c] += 1 }
    
    total_triples = 0
    
    char_counts.each do |char, count|
        if count >= 3
            total_triples += (count * (count - 1) * (count - 2)) / 6
        end
    end
    
    left_counts = Hash.new(0)
    right_counts = Hash.new(0)
    
    B.each_char { |c| right_counts[c] += 1 }
    
    (0...n).each do |j|
        mid_char = B[j]
        right_counts[mid_char] -= 1
        
        if j > 0 && j < n - 1
            left_counts.each do |char, l_count|
                if char != mid_char
                    r_count = right_counts[char]
                    if r_count > 0
                        total_triples += l_count * r_count
                    end
                end
            end
        end
        
        left_counts[mid_char] += 1
    end
    
    total_triples
end

from collections import deque

def main():
    n = int(input())
    students = []
    groups = [[], [], []]
    
    for i in range(n):
        g, s = map(int, input().split())
        students.append((g-1, s))
        groups[g-1].append(s)
    
    total_skill = sum(s for _, s in students)
    
    if total_skill % 3 != 0:
        print(-1)
        return
    
    target = total_skill
    

    current_skills = [sum(group) for group in groups]
    
    if all(skill == target for skill in current_skills):
        print(0)
        return
    



    
    queue = deque([(current_skills[0], current_skills[1], 0)])
    visited = set()
    visited.add((current_skills[0], current_skills[1]))
    
    while queue:
        skill1, skill2, moves = queue.popleft()
        skill3 = total_skill - skill1 - skill2
        
        if skill1 == target and skill2 == target and skill3 == target:
            print(moves)
            return
        

        for i, (orig_group, student_skill) in enumerate(students):
            current_group_skills = [skill1, skill2, skill3]
            

            for new_group in range(3):
                if new_group == orig_group:
                    continue
                

                new_skills = current_group_skills[:]
                new_skills[orig_group] -= student_skill
                new_skills[new_group] += student_skill
                

                if all(s >= 0 for s in new_skills):
                    state = (new_skills[0], new_skills[1])
                    if state not in visited:
                        visited.add(state)
                        queue.append((new_skills[0], new_skills[1], moves + 1))
    
    print(-1)

def main()
  n = gets.to_i
  students = []
  
  n.times do |i|
    g, s = gets.split.map(&:to_i)
    students << [g, s]
  end
  
  total_skill = students.sum { |g, s| s }
  
  if total_skill % 3 != 0
    puts -1
    return
  end
  
  target = total_skill / 3
  

  min_switches = Float::INFINITY
  
  def backtrack(students, assignment, group_sums, target, switches, min_switches, index)
    if index == students.length
      if group_sums[1] == target && group_sums[2] == target && group_sums[3] == target
        return [switches, min_switches].min
      else
        return min_switches
      end
    end
    
    original_group, skill = students[index]
    

    (1..3).each do |group|
      if group_sums[group] + skill <= target
        group_sums[group] += skill
        new_switches = switches + (group == original_group ? 0 : 1)
        
        if new_switches < min_switches
          min_switches = backtrack(students, assignment + [group], group_sums, target, new_switches, min_switches, index + 1)
        end
        
        group_sums[group] -= skill
      end
    end
    
    min_switches
  end
  
  result = backtrack(students, [], {1 => 0, 2 => 0, 3 => 0}, target, 0, min_switches, 0)
  
  if result == Float::INFINITY
    puts -1
  else
    puts result
  end
end

  n.times do
    g, s = gets.split.map(&:to_i)
    students << [g, s]
    total_skill += s

  if total_skill % 3 != 0
    puts -1

  target = total_skill / 3
  
  dp = Array.new(target + 1) { Array.new(target + 1, Float::INFINITY) }
  dp[0][0] = 0

  students.each do |g, s|
    new_dp = Array.new(target + 1) { Array.new(target + 1, Float::INFINITY) }
    (0..target).each do |s1|
      (0..target).each do |s2|
        next if dp[s1][s2] == Float::INFINITY
        
        cost1 = (g == 1 ? 0 : 1)
        if s1 + s <= target
          new_val = dp[s1][s2] + cost1
          new_dp[s1 + s][s2] = new_val if new_val < new_dp[s1 + s][s2]
        end

        cost2 = (g == 2 ? 0 : 1)
        if s2 + s <= target
          new_val = dp[s1][s2] + cost2
          new_dp[s1][s2 + s] = new_val if new_val < new_dp[s1][s2 + s]
        end

        cost3 = (g == 3 ? 0 : 1)
        new_val = dp[s1][s2] + cost3
        new_dp[s1][s2] = new_val if new_val < new_dp[s1][s2]
      end
    end
    dp = new_dp
  end

  ans = dp[target][target]
  if ans == Float::INFINITY
    puts -1
  else
    puts ans
  end

        if active[i]
          a, b, c = connections[i]
          adj[a] << [b, c]
          adj[b] << [a, c]
        end
      end
      

      dist = Array.new(n + 1, Float::INFINITY)
      dist[x] = 0
      visited = Array.new(n + 1, false)
      
      n.times do

        u = -1
        (1..n).each do |v|
          if !visited[v] && (u == -1 || dist[v] < dist[u])
            u = v
          end
        end
        
        break if u == -1 || dist[u] == Float::INFINITY
        visited[u] = true
        
        adj[u].each do |v, w|
          if !visited[v]
            new_dist = [dist[u], w].max
            if new_dist < dist[v]
              dist[v] = new_dist
            end
          end
        end
      end
      
      if dist[y] == Float::INFINITY
        puts -1
      else
        puts dist[y]
      end
    end
  end
end

def main
  n, m, q = gets.split.map(&:to_i)
  edges = []
  m.times do
    edges << gets.split.map(&:to_i)
  end

  queries = []
  removed_indices = []
  q.times do |i|
    query = gets.split.map(&:to_i)
    queries << query
    removed_indices << query[1] - 1 if query[0] == 1
  end

  is_removed = Array.new(m, false)
  removed_indices.each { |idx| is_removed[idx] = true }

  dist = Array.new(n + 1) { Array.new(n + 1, Float::INFINITY) }
  (1..n).each { |i| dist[i][i] = 0 }

  edges.each_with_index do |(u, v, c), i|
    unless is_removed[i]
      dist[u][v] = dist[v][u] = [dist[u][v], c].min
    end
  end

  def floyd_warshall(n, dist)
    (1..n).each do |k|
      (1..n).each do |i|
        next if dist[i][k] == Float::INFINITY
        (1..n).each do |j|
          if dist[i][j] > dist[i][k] + dist[k][j]
            dist[i][j] = dist[i][k] + dist[k][j]
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end

  floyd_warshall(n, dist)

  results = []
  queries.reverse_each do |query|
    if query[0] == 1
      idx = query[1] - 1
      u, v, c = edges[idx]
      if dist[u][v] > c
        dist[u][v] = dist[v][u] = c
        (1..n).each do |i|
          (1..n).each do |j|
            new_dist = [dist[i][u] + c + dist[v][j], dist[i][v] + c + dist[u][j]].min
            if dist[i][j] > new_dist
              dist[i][j] = new_dist
            end
          end
        end
      end
    else
      x, y = query[1], query[2]
      results << (dist[x][y] == Float::INFINITY ? -1 : dist[x][y])
    end
  end

  puts results.reverse
end

require 'set'

def dijkstra(graph, start, target, n)
  dist = Array.new(n + 1, Float::INFINITY)
  dist[start] = 0
  pq = [[0, start]]
  visited = Set.new
  
  until pq.empty?
    current_dist, u = pq.shift
    next if visited.include?(u)
    visited.add(u)
    
    return current_dist if u == target
    
    next unless graph[u]
    
    graph[u].each do |v, weight|
      next if visited.include?(v)
      new_dist = current_dist + weight
      if new_dist < dist[v]
        dist[v] = new_dist
        pq << [new_dist, v]
        pq.sort!
      end
    end
  end
  
  Float::INFINITY
end

def build_graph(connections, exclude_idx = -1)
  graph = {}
  connections.each_with_index do |(u, v, w), idx|
    next if idx == exclude_idx
    graph[u] ||= []
    graph[v] ||= []
    graph[u] << [v, w]
    graph[v] << [u, w]
  end
  graph
end

  connections = []
  
  m.times do
    u, v, w = gets.split.map(&:to_i)
    connections << [u, v, w]
  end
  

  full_graph = build_graph(connections)
  full_min = dijkstra(full_graph, 1, n, n)
  

  m.times do |i|
    partial_graph = build_graph(connections, i)
    partial_min = dijkstra(partial_graph, 1, n, n)
    
    if full_min != partial_min
      puts "Yes"
    else
      puts "No"
    end
  end
end

import sys
import heapq

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data:
        return
    
    N = int(input_data[0])
    M = int(input_data[1])
    
    adj = [[] for _ in range(N + 1)]
    edges = []
    idx = 2
    for i in range(M):
        u = int(input_data[idx])
        v = int(input_data[idx+1])
        w = int(input_data[idx+2])
        adj[u].append((v, w, i))
        adj[v].append((u, w, i))
        edges.append((u, v, w))
        idx += 3

    def dijkstra(start_node):
        dist = [float('inf')] * (N + 1)
        dist[start_node] = 0
        pq = [(0, start_node)]
        while pq:
            d, u = heapq.heappop(pq)
            if d > dist[u]:
                continue
            for v, w, i in adj[u]:
                if dist[v] > d + w:
                    dist[v] = d + w
                    heapq.heappush(pq, (dist[v], v))
        return dist

    dist1 = dijkstra(1)
    distN = dijkstra(N)
    
    min_dist = dist1[N]
    
    dag_adj = [[] for _ in range(N + 1)]
    dag_rev = [[] for _ in range(N + 1)]
    is_on_shortest = [False] * M
    
    for i in range(M):
        u, v, w = edges[i]
        if dist1[u] + w + distN[v] == min_dist:
            dag_adj[u].append((v, i))
            dag_rev[v].append((u, i))
            is_on_shortest[i] = True
        elif dist1[v] + w + distN[u] == min_dist:
            dag_adj[v].append((u, i))
            dag_rev[u].append((v, i))
            is_on_shortest[i] = True

    tin = [0] * (N + 1)
    low = [0] * (N + 1)
    timer = 0
    bridges = set()

    def find_bridges():
        nonlocal timer
        visited = [False] * (N + 1)
        
        stack = [(1, -1, iter(dag_adj[1]))]
        visited[1] = True
        timer += 1
        tin[1] = low[1] = timer
        
        while stack:
            u, p_idx, it = stack[-1]
            try:
                v, idx = next(it)
                if idx == p_idx:
                    continue
                if visited[v]:
                    low[u] = min(low[u], tin[v])
                else:
                    visited[v] = True
                    timer += 1
                    tin[v] = low[v] = timer
                    stack.append((v, idx, iter(dag_adj[v])))
            except StopIteration:
                stack.pop()
                if stack:
                    p_u, _, _ = stack[-1]
                    low[p_u] = min(low[p_u], low[u])
                    if low[u] > tin[p_u]:
                        bridges.add(p_idx)

    find_bridges()
    
    results = []
    for i in range(M):
        if i in bridges:
            results.append("Yes")
        else:
            results.append("No")
    
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(results) + '\n')

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()

def min_distance(from, to, n, blocked)
  return 0 if from == to
  

  clockwise = 0
  pos = from
  valid_clockwise = true
  
  while pos != to
    pos = pos % n + 1
    clockwise += 1
    if pos == blocked
      valid_clockwise = false
      break
    end
  end
  

  counterclockwise = 0
  pos = from
  valid_counterclockwise = true
  
  while pos != to
    pos = pos - 1
    pos = n if pos == 0
    counterclockwise += 1
    if pos == blocked
      valid_counterclockwise = false
      break
    end
  end
  
  if valid_clockwise && valid_counterclockwise
    [clockwise, counterclockwise].min
  elsif valid_clockwise
    clockwise
  elsif valid_counterclockwise
    counterclockwise
  else

    Float::INFINITY
  end
end

      total_operations += operations
      left_pos = target
    else
      operations = min_distance(right_pos, target, n, left_pos)
      total_operations += operations
      right_pos = target
    end
  end
  
  puts total_operations
end

                check = (check % N) + 1
                if check == blocker:
                    can_go_cw = False
                    break
            
            if can_go_cw:
                total_moves += dist_cw
            else:
                total_moves += dist_ccw

    if h == 'L'
      total_moves += get_dist(n, l_pos, t, r_pos)
      l_pos = t
    else
      total_moves += get_dist(n, r_pos, t, l_pos)
      r_pos = t
    end
  end
  
  puts total_moves
end

def get_dist(n, start, target, block)
  cw_dist = 0
  curr = start
  possible_cw = true
  while curr != target
    curr = curr + 1
    curr = 1 if curr > n
    cw_dist += 1
    if curr == block
      possible_cw = false
      break
    end
  end
  
  ccw_dist = 0
  curr = start
  possible_ccw = true
  while curr != target
    curr = curr - 1
    curr = n if curr < 1
    ccw_dist += 1
    if curr == block
      possible_ccw = false
      break
    end
  end
  
  if possible_cw && possible_ccw
    return [cw_dist, ccw_dist].min
  elsif possible_cw
    return cw_dist
  else
    return ccw_dist
  end
end

    return false unless assigned
  end
  
  true

def solve():
    n, k = map(int, input().split())
    D = list(map(int, input().split()))
    E = list(map(int, input().split()))
    

    items = list(zip(D, E))
    

    unique_durabilities = sorted(set(D))
    
    min_result = float('inf')
    
    for max_dur in unique_durabilities:

        candidates = []
        items_with_max_dur = []
        
        for dur, eff in items:
            if dur <= max_dur:
                candidates.append(eff)
                if dur == max_dur:
                    items_with_max_dur.append(eff)
        
        if len(candidates) < k:
            continue
            

        candidates.sort()
        items_with_max_dur.sort()
        


        min_eff_at_max = items_with_max_dur[0]
        

        remaining = []
        used_min_at_max = False
        
        for eff in candidates:
            if eff == min_eff_at_max and not used_min_at_max:
                used_min_at_max = True
                continue
            remaining.append(eff)
        
        if len(remaining) >= k - 1:
            remaining.sort()
            total_eff = min_eff_at_max + sum(remaining[:k-1])
            result = max_dur * total_eff
            min_result = min(min_result, result)
    
    return min_result

def main():
    t = int(input())
    for _ in range(t):
        print(solve())

import sys
import heapq

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data:
        return
    
    idx = 0
    T_str = input_data[idx]
    idx += 1
    T = int(T_str)
    
    output = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(input_data[idx])
        K = int(input_data[idx+1])
        idx += 2
        
        D = list(map(int, input_data[idx:idx+N]))
        idx += N
        E = list(map(int, input_data[idx:idx+N]))
        idx += N
        
        pairs = sorted(zip(D, E), key=lambda x: x[0])
        
        min_val = float('inf')
        current_sum_E = 0
        min_heap = []
        
        for i in range(N):
            d_i, e_i = pairs[i]
            
            if len(min_heap) < K:
                heapq.heappush(min_heap, -e_i)
                current_sum_E += e_i
            else:
                if e_i < -min_heap[0]:
                    max_e = -heapq.heapreplace(min_heap, -e_i)
                    current_sum_E = current_sum_E - max_e + e_i
            
            if len(min_heap) == K:
                val = d_i * current_sum_E
                if val < min_val:
                    min_val = val
                    
        output.append(str(min_val))
    
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(output) + "\n")

class MinHeap
  def initialize
    @heap = []
  end

  def push(val)
    @heap << val
    index = @heap.size - 1
    while index > 0
      parent = (index - 1) / 2
      break if @heap[parent] <= @heap[index]
      @heap[parent], @heap[index] = @heap[index], @heap[parent]
      index = parent
    end
  end

  def pop
    return nil if @heap.empty?
    return @heap.pop if @heap.size == 1
    root = @heap[0]
    @heap[0] = @heap.pop
    index = 0
    while (left = 2 * index + 1) < @heap.size
      right = left + 1
      min_child = (right < @heap.size && @heap[right] < @heap[left]) ? right : left
      break if @heap[index] <= @heap[min_child]
      @heap[index], @heap[min_child] = @heap[min_child], @heap[index]
      index = min_child
    end
    root
  end

  def size
    @heap.size
  end

  def min
    @heap[0]
  end
end

    tools.sort_by! { |t| t[0] }
    
    heap = MinHeap.new
    current_sum = 0
    min_total = Float::INFINITY
    
    tools.each do |d, e|
      heap.push(e)
      current_sum += e
      
      if heap.size > k
        removed = heap.pop
        current_sum -= removed
      end
      
      if heap.size == k
        score = d * current_sum
        min_total = score if score < min_total
      end
    end
    results << min_total
  end
  puts results
end

def circular_distance(from, to, n)
  clockwise = (to - from) % n
  counterclockwise = (from - to) % n
  [clockwise, counterclockwise].min
end

def solve_instruction(left, right, target_hand, target_pos, n)
  if target_hand == 'L'
    current_pos = left
    other_pos = right
  else
    current_pos = right
    other_pos = left
  end
  
  return 0 if current_pos == target_pos
  

  min_ops = Float::INFINITY
  

  ops = 0
  pos = current_pos
  blocked = false
  while pos != target_pos
    next_pos = pos % n + 1
    if next_pos == other_pos
      blocked = true
      break
    end
    pos = next_pos
    ops += 1
  end
  min_ops = [min_ops, ops].min unless blocked
  

  ops = 0
  pos = current_pos
  blocked = false
  while pos != target_pos
    next_pos = pos == 1 ? n : pos - 1
    if next_pos == other_pos
      blocked = true
      break
    end
    pos = next_pos
    ops += 1
  end
  min_ops = [min_ops, ops].min unless blocked
  

  if min_ops == Float::INFINITY


    best_total = Float::INFINITY
    
    (1..n).each do |new_other_pos|
      next if new_other_pos == current_pos || new_other_pos == target_pos
      

      other_move_cost = circular_distance(other_pos, new_other_pos, n)
      

      target_move_cost = circular_distance(current_pos, target_pos, n)
      
      total_cost = other_move_cost + target_move_cost
      best_total = [best_total, total_cost].min
    end
    
    min_ops = best_total
  end
  
  min_ops
end

MOD = 998244353

def mod_inverse(a, mod = MOD)
  pow(a, mod - 2, mod)
end

def solve_case
  n = gets.to_i
  parents = gets.split.map(&:to_i)
  a = gets.split.map(&:to_i)
  
  total_a = a.sum
  

  children = Array.new(n + 1) { [] }
  (1..n).each do |i|
    children[parents[i - 1]].push(i)
  end
  

  memo = {}
  

  def expected_ops(allocated_mask, children, a, total_a, memo)
    return memo[allocated_mask] if memo.has_key?(allocated_mask)
    

    prob_done = 0
    (1...a.length).each do |i|
      if (allocated_mask & (1 << i)) != 0
        prob_done = (prob_done + a[i]) % MOD
      end
    end
    
    if prob_done == total_a % MOD
      return memo[allocated_mask] = 0
    end
    

    available = []
    (1...a.length).each do |i|
      next if (allocated_mask & (1 << i)) != 0
      parent = i == 1 ? 0 : nil

      (0...a.length).each do |j|
        if j == 0
          next if i == 1
        else
          next if children[j].nil? || !children[j].include?(i)
        end
        parent = j
        break
      end
      

      if parent == 0 || (allocated_mask & (1 << parent)) != 0
        available.push(i)
      end
    end
    
    return memo[allocated_mask] = 0 if available.empty?
    

    min_cost = nil
    
    available.each do |dept|
      new_mask = allocated_mask | (1 << dept)
      

      prob_this = a[dept - 1]

      prob_continue = (total_a - prob_done - prob_this) % MOD
      
      if prob_continue == 0
        cost = 1
      else
        future_cost = expected_ops(new_mask, children, a, total_a, memo)

        prob_total_remaining = (total_a - prob_done) % MOD
        cost = (1 + prob_continue * mod_inverse(prob_total_remaining) % MOD * future_cost % MOD) % MOD
      end
      
      min_cost = cost if min_cost.nil? || cost < min_cost
    end
    
    memo[allocated_mask] = min_cost || 0
  end
  

  parents_map = {}
  (1..n).each do |i|
    parents_map[i] = parents

def solve():

        S_A = 0
        for i in range(1, N + 1):
            A[i] = int(input_data[idx])
            idx += 1
            S_A += A[i]
            
        subtree_sum = [0] * (N + 1)
        for i in range(N, 0, -1):
            subtree_sum[i] += A[i]
            subtree_sum[P[i]] += subtree_sum[i]
            
        inv_cache = {}
        
        def get_inv(n):
            if n in inv_cache:
                return inv_cache[n]
            res = pow(n, MOD - 2, MOD)
            inv_cache[n] = res
            return res

        total_expected_sum = 0
        for i in range(1, N + 1):
            val = (A[i] * get_inv(subtree_sum[i])) % MOD
            total_expected_sum = (total_expected_sum + val) % MOD
            
        ans = (total_expected_sum * (S_A % MOD)) % MOD
        results.append(str(ans))
        
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(results) + '\n')

MOD = 998244353

def power(a, b)
  res = 1
  a %= MOD
  while b > 0
    res = (res * a) % MOD if b % 2 == 1
    a = (a * a) % MOD
    b /= 2
  end
  res
end

def inverse(n)
  power(n, MOD - 2)
end

  t = input[idx]
  idx += 1
  
  t.times do
    n = input[idx]
    idx += 1
    p_arr = [0] * (n + 1)
    adj = Array.new(n + 1) { [] }
    (1..n).each do |i|
      p_arr[i] = input[idx]
      adj[p_arr[i]] << i
      idx += 1
    end
    a = [0] * (n + 1)
    sum_a = 0
    (1..n).each do |i|
      a[i] = input[idx]
      sum_a += a[i]
      idx += 1
    end
    
    subtree_sum = a.dup
    (n).downto(1) do |i|
      subtree_sum[p_arr[i]] += subtree_sum[i] if i > 0
    end
    
    total_expected = 0
    inv_sum_a = inverse(sum_a % MOD)
    
    (1..n).each do |i|
      prob_i = (a[i] * inv_sum_a) % MOD
      
      term = 1
      curr = p_arr[i]
      while curr != 0
        s_u = subtree_sum[curr]
        s_v = subtree_sum[i]
        
        val = (s_v * inverse(s_u)) % MOD
        term = (term + val) % MOD
        
        i = curr
        curr = p_arr[i]
      end
      
      total_expected = (total_expected + prob_i * term) % MOD
    end
    
    puts total_expected
  end
end

    a = input[idx]
    b = input[idx + 1]
    idx += 2
    if b < min_b[a]
      min_b[a] = b
    end
  end

  (c).downto(1) do |i|
    suffix_min[i] = min_b[i] < suffix_min[i + 1] ? min_b[i] : suffix_min[i + 1]
  end
  
  ans = 0
  r_limit = c
  (1..c).each do |x|
    r_limit = suffix_min[x] - 1
    if r_limit >= x
      ans += (r_limit - x + 1)
    end
  end
  
  puts ans
end

    D = [d - 1 for d in D]
    

    original = D[:]
    current = D[:]
    cycle_length = 0
    
    while True:
        cycle_length += 1

        new_deck = [0] * N
        for i in range(N):
            new_deck[i] = current[current[i]]
        current = new_deck
        
        if current == original:
            break
    

    effective_k = K % cycle_length
    

    result = D[:]
    for _ in range(effective_k):
        new_deck = [0] * N
        for i in range(N):
            new_deck[i] = result[result[i]]
        result = new_deck
    

    result = [r + 1 for r in result]
    print(' '.join(map(str, result)))

            shift = pow(2, k, m)
            for idx in range(m):
                res[cycle[idx]] = cycle[(idx + shift) % m]
    
    print(*(x + 1 for x in res))

import sys

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data:
        return
    N = int(input_data[0])
    M = int(input_data[1])
    
    rows = set()
    cols = set()
    diag1 = set()
    diag2 = set()
    
    for k in range(M):
        r = int(input_data[2 + 2 * k])
        c = int(input_data[3 + 2 * k])
        rows.add(r)
        cols.add(c)
        diag1.add(r + c)
        diag2.add(r - c)
    
    sorted_rows = sorted(list(rows))
    sorted_cols = sorted(list(cols))
    sorted_diag1 = sorted(list(diag1))
    sorted_diag2 = sorted(list(diag2))
    
    total_cells = N * N
    
    m_r = len(sorted_rows)
    m_c = len(sorted_cols)
    m_d1 = len(sorted_diag1)
    m_d2 = len(sorted_diag2)
    
    covered = m_r * N + m_c * N - m_r * m_c
    
    remaining_rows = []
    last = 0
    for r in sorted_rows:
        if r > last + 1:
            remaining_rows.append((last + 1, r - 1))
        last = r
    if last < N:
        remaining_rows.append((last + 1, N))
        
    remaining_cols = []
    last = 0
    for c in sorted_cols:
        if c > last + 1:
            remaining_cols.append((last + 1, c - 1))
        last = c
    if last < N:
        remaining_cols.append((last + 1, N))

    def count_in_rect(d1_set, d2_set, r_range, c_range):
        r_start, r_end = r_range
        c_start, c_end = c_range
        
        points = []
        for d1 in d1_set:
            for d2 in d2_set:
                if (d1 + d2) % 2 == 0:
                    r = (d1 + d2)
                    c = (d1 - d2)
                    if r_start <= r <= r_end and c_start <= c <= c_end:
                        points.append((r, c))
        
        count = 0
        for d1 in d1_set:
            min_c = max(c_start, d1 - r_end)
            max_c = min(c_end, d1 - r_start)
            if min_c <= max_c:
                count += (max_c - min_c + 1)
        
        for d2 in d2_set:
            min_c = max(c_start, r_start - d2)
            max_c = min(c_end, r_end - d2)
            if min_c <= max_c:
                count += (max_c - min_c + 1)
        
        count -= len(set(points))
        return count

    ans = 0
    for rr in remaining_rows:
        for rc in remaining_cols:
            ans += count_in_rect(sorted_diag1, sorted_diag2, rr, rc)
            
    print(total_cells - covered - ans)

def main():
    solve()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

def main()
  n = gets.to_i
  documents = []
  
  n.times do |i|
    documents << gets.chomp
    
    if i == 0

      puts documents[0].length
    else
      current = documents[i]
      min_cost = current.length
      

      (0...i).each do |j|
        target = documents[j]
        

        common_prefix_len = 0
        min_len = [current.length, target.length].min
        
        (0...min_len).each do |k|
          if current[k] == target[k]
            common_prefix_len += 1
          else
            break
          end
        end
        

        deletions = current.length - common_prefix_len
        additions = target.length - common_prefix_len
        cost = deletions + additions
        
        min_cost = [min_cost, cost].min
      end
      
      puts min_cost
    end
  end
end

def solve
  input = $stdin.read.split
  return if input.empty?
  n = input[0].to_i
  docs = input[1..n]

  trie = [[-1] * 26]
  depths = [0]
  
  results = []
  
  docs.each_with_index do |d, i|
    len = d.length
    if i == 0
      results << len
    else
      curr = 0
      max_prefix_depth = 0
      d.each_byte do |b|
        char_idx = b - 97
        next_node = trie[curr][char_idx]
        break if next_node == -1
        curr = next_node
        max_prefix_depth = depths[curr]
      end
      
      results << (len - max_prefix_depth + 1)
    end
    
    curr = 0
    d.each_byte do |b|
      char_idx = b - 97
      if trie[curr][char_idx] == -1
        trie[curr][char_idx] = trie.size
        depths << depths[curr] + 1
        trie << ([-1] * 26)
      end
      curr = trie[curr][char_idx]
    end
  end
  
  puts results
end

def main
  solve
end

import sys

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data:
        return
    
    idx = 0
    N = int(input_data[idx])
    idx += 1
    
    services = []
    for _ in range(N):
        q = int(input_data[idx])
        r = int(input_data[idx+1])
        services.append((q, r))
        idx += 2
        
    Q = int(input_data[idx])
    idx += 1
    
    results = []
    for _ in range(Q):
        t = int(input_data[idx]) - 1
        d = int(input_data[idx+1])
        idx += 2
        
        q, r = services[t]
        
        current_rem = d % q
        if current_rem <= r:
            ans = d + (r - current_rem)
        else:
            ans = d + (q - current_rem + r)
            
        results.append(str(ans))
        
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(results) + "\n")

  h, w, k = gets.split.map(&:to_i)
  grid = []
  h.times do
    grid << gets.chomp
  end
  
  directions = [[-1, 0], [1, 0], [0, -1], [0, 1]]
  total_count = 0
  
  def dfs(grid, h, w, k, row, col, visited, directions, moves_left)
    if moves_left == 0
      return 1
    end
    
    count = 0
    directions.each do |dr, dc|
      new_row = row + dr
      new_col = col + dc
      
      if new_row >= 0 && new_row < h && new_col >= 0 && new_col < w &&
         grid[new_row][new_col] == 'O' && !visited[new_row][new_col]
        
        visited[new_row][new_col] = true
        count += dfs(grid, h, w, k, new_row, new_col, visited, directions, moves_left - 1)
        visited[new_row][new_col] = false
      end
    end
    
    count
  end
  
  (0...h).each do |i|
    (0...w).each do |j|
      if grid[i][j] == 'O'
        visited = Array.new(h) { Array.new(w, false) }
        visited[i][j] = true
        total_count += dfs(grid, h, w, k, i, j, visited, directions, k)
      end
    end
  end
  
  puts total_count
end

def dfs(r, c, depth)
  if depth == $k
    $count += 1
    return
  end

  [[r + 1, c], [r - 1, c], [r, c + 1], [r, c - 1]].each do |nr, nc|
    if nr >= 0 && nr < $h && nc >= 0 && nc < $w && $grid[nr][nc] == 'O' && !$visited[nr][nc]
      $visited[nr][nc] = true
      dfs(nr, nc, depth + 1)
      $visited[nr][nc] = false
    end
  end
end
